In the spring of 2020, the world faced an unprecedented global crisis. During this challenging time, I found myself confronted with a deeply personal struggle: the end of my 20-year marriage. With two school-aged children to prioritize, I sought strength, guidance, and expert legal representation. I found all of that—and more—in Margo Fox and her outstanding team.

Navigating the complexities of family law during the pandemic presented unique challenges. Courts were closed, remote hearings were new territory, and legal processes faced significant delays. Despite these obstacles, Margo Fox demonstrated exceptional professionalism and adaptability. She took the time to explain every step of the process, often more than once, and ensured her team was thoroughly prepared for every meeting, filing, and court appearance.

Over the course of the case, we faced numerous setbacks, including the replacement of opposing counsel on three separate occasions. These changes repeatedly delayed progress and required us to reset the process each time. Yet, Margo remained steadfast, organized, and proactive in addressing every challenge that arose.

After more than two years of negotiations, document collection, and navigating external factors like housing market fluctuations and logistical issues, we reached an agreement. By December 2022—33 months after the case began—the court order was finalized. However, there were additional documents related to property matters that required further attention. Without opposing counsel, the final steps moved slowly. Margo reassured me throughout this phase, and together we decided to let the process proceed naturally. Once the mortgage was satisfied in November 2024, the case was officially closed in December 2024.

Margo’s intelligence, extensive legal knowledge, and years of experience were evident at every stage. She approached every situation with calm confidence, ensuring I felt supported and informed throughout this difficult time. Her dedication and expertise set her apart as an exceptional attorney.

If you are seeking a skilled, compassionate, and highly capable lawyer, look no further than Margo Fox. She and her team will provide you with the guidance and care you need. I couldn’t be more grateful for their support during one of the most challenging periods of my life.

I hold the the utmost respect for Ms. Fox as an exceedingly knowledgeable and professional attorney, and for who she is as a person. Margo and her team are extraordinary. In my first visit to her office — during what was one of the most difficult periods in my life — she placed a priority on my overall wellbeing, not just the legal aspects of my situation. Her faith, dedication, responsiveness, and caring was nothing shy of an absolute blessing. It became immediately obvious during our initial meeting that Margot and her team were the exact right fit for my legal needs. Throughout, I was working with someone that had a consistent, all-encompassing concern for my best interests. Fox Law is an amazing law team. And they’re even better people.

Her law firm possesses a profound understanding of the legal landscape, which is evident in their ability to effectively navigate complex cases. Their expertise  is unparalleled, and their guidance was invaluable in helping me navigate the complexities of my situation.
She's best out there hands down!

Don't believe the hype that they are truly here to help African Americans-Blacks. Reviewing their website its covered with Civil Rights and so forth, but its all a lie.

Here is my case;

Today is the last day to file my lawsuit and I have no attorney and I wasted two years and one month truly thinking that you (Attorney Esha Rajendran) and the firm (Ellwanger Law) you work for truly had my best interest. I spoke with now 50+ attorneys in the DFW area and all asked the same question, why were you dropped from the firm that represented you through the EEOC process. All of the attorneys advised it wasn’t enough to support why I was dropped, especially if I received representation for two years.

I even tried reaching back out to you (Attorney Esha Rajendran) for additional assistance and received nothing. Thank you (Attorney Esha Rajendran) and your firm (Ellwanger Law) for no help. I am devastated and even got treated for depression. You all truly failed me.

As stated, keep moving and find an attorney that look like you, instead of seeking representation from someone that is pretending to help African Americans-Blacks. We are being sought and misguided by these people.

reviews.text: I always seek The Best, so I definitely didn't want to hire one of those "Good Ole Boy" local defense attorneys, who goes fishing with the DA on the weekend, and have my fate compromised or decided on a lake. So I did a search for the top Defense Attorneys in Texas, and Mr. Torberson's name was in the top of all the lists, and as I read his profile and researched past cases, I was quite impressed with one case in particular. In June 2016, Mr. Torberson took on a case to represent and protect six dogs from being euthanized after attacking and killing a Process Server, who trespassed and ignored a "Beware Of Dogs " sign at a Manor, Texas residence. It was at that moment that I knew he was the man I wanted and needed to represent me and handle my defense; a man, who truly loves animals, as you will see, when you visit his office and meet his beautiful pets in person and in photos. My point? Well, a man dedicated to insuring an animal's right to proper representation and a fair trial will definitely fight just as hard and determined, if not harder, for a human.....and he did just that in my case. I have been in and out of the judicial system for many issues and have been represented by numerous attorneys; however, I can definitely say without an ounce of doubt that Eric Torberson is one of the Best. I could use an infinite number of positive adjectives to describe him and his approach to handling a case and the utilization of the law, but I will just leave it at this...You're a damn fool if you fail to hire this man for representation in any legal matter.
reviews.ownerResponse: Thanks Russell, that’s very kind.

reviews.text: I am so very grateful to have had Eric represent me. I was extremely anxious and he kept me informed every step of the way. What I appreciate most is how responsive and approachable he is—he always answers calls and emails quickly and never leaves me guessing about next steps. He breaks things down so they actually make sense, and I always feel confident knowing he’s in my corner.

If you want an attorney who’s knowledgeable, dependable, and genuinely cares about his clients’ success, James is the one I’d recommend every time.

“I’ve never heard of something like this so my office wouldn’t want to take this case, best of luck”… — so because you don’t know something you’re not willing to offer guidance and help, or even spend time learning about it? I would think someone who does this much schooling would enjoy learning.
In the consultation he asked no follow up questions or anything. Just cold and stoic.

Best of luck to you, too.

I’ve worked with other attorneys in the Austin area and my opinion, he is the best one I’ve worked with because of his level of service. I cannot say enough good things about my experience and would encourage anyone who needs help navigating the complex world of real estate to make him your first call.

I called Blake today and I cannot put into words how grateful I am to have come across his phone number online. I felt so lost and confused for months( actually years) and this gentleman in a short 5-10 minute conversation answered every single question I have ever had about my family’s property in Texas. He was so honest, knowledgeable and didn’t even once mention anything about charging me for the information that he was providing. It’s not everyday that people are willing to help you out and Blake saved my family so much time without expecting anything in return. I highly recommend him. Thank you, Blake!

I CALLED REGARDING ASSISTANCE IN TERMINATION OF A RESIDENTIAL LEASE FOUR MONTHS EARLY. THE ATTORNEY WAS VERY HONEST. HE SAID HE CANNOT HELP ME IN THIS MATTER AND HE DOES NOT WANT TO TAKE MY MONEY. FURTHERMORE, HE OFFERED SUGGESTIONS.  HE SPENT ABOUT 10 MINUTES GOING OVER SCENARIOS.

I called The Blake Law Firm to get a better understanding of what hiring a real estate lawyer would entail if i were to sell my house myself vs  getting an agent. The first thing I was told was the negative side  of selling myself. Then I was given an opinion of how the market is doing and what the outcome of selling myself vs using an agent would be. The man I spoke to, which I did not get a name, was very honest and gave me much more information than I expected. He also gave the price for her services and the pros i would receive along with the price he charges vs the price an agent charges. I haven't used his services yet but if I do need a lawyer he will be the person I call. Awesome experience. Thanks.

James is amazing.  I called him, he called back almost immediately.  He listened to everything I was needing, which was a lot, and then he laid it out very simply what he needed from me to make it happen.  I will not get into the details of everything I needed, but lets just say after much bad advice, it was a lot.  He explained to me, in layman's terms, why I needed what he was suggesting to me and then how to make it happen.  If I could give him 10 starts I would. I am quite demanding and James handled it like an absolute professional.  I would recommend him 1000X times over.

We are sorry that we needed to seek the help of an attorney, but happy that when we did, we were able to find James Blake.  James answered the phone from my first frustrated phone call where I explained that after having a lease for our business in Northwest Austin for 24 years and in this particular building for 10 years, we suddenly ran into issues with our new landlords (our building had been sold to a new out-of-state company) and apparently they read our lease differently than we and our previous landlords did.  I tried via phone calls, letters, and ultimately return receipt requested letters to discus and resolve the issue on my own, but after 5 months of receiving no reasonable response, much less resolution, we decided we needed a lawyer.

First, I just wanted to know if he felt we were interpreting the lease correctly.  I am not a lawyer and a lease contains some rather complicated language, so I wanted to start there with a professional.  He felt that we were reading it correctly and said that he and his colleges are seeing our same issue happening quite a bit all of the sudden Austin, probably due to the rapid growth.

We had never personally or professionally had a dispute requiring legal counsel before so this was all very new to us.  James walked us through every step of the process and did quite a bit of background research for our position.  Ultimately, once we had legal counsel and after months of back and forth, our landlords (and their team of lawyers) acquiesced and we were able to get reimbursed for the overages that we had paid per their demand and that moving forward we would be assessed the rate in our contract.  He was genuinely happy for us when it all resolved stating "love it when the good guys win."  Well, the good guys were getting no where before calling on his expertise.  I can't recommend him enough.
If I could give 0 i would

Wow, I just called him to ask if he does reviews for contracts. He was kind enough to ask me "what state and county"....he pointed me in the direction to get an attorney in Michigan so I would not have to pay travel fees "if" I was to be called to court.....who does that? This guy!!!
What a solid individual and what a very, very rare find in the world now. He is now saved in my contacts and if I was to need anything in the Austin area that fall within his wheel house I'm heading here.

I would highly recommend James Blake to anyone seeking legal counsel for small business related issues. James has advised me on a multitude of issues since I started my company, including but not limited to: business startup and formation, negotiating business contracts, putting in place independent contractor agreements and work orders, intellectual property advisement, non-competition agreements, lead generation, and general business development advice. He is extremely accessible by phone and email, is always quick to respond. When he has given an estimated date for a deliverable, he always delivers on time or early. In addition, he has taken the time to get to know me and my business, and understand some of the highly technical work that I do. As a technical consultant, it was important to have someone who could help take the technical work that I do and translate that into a business model that is attractive to potential clients. Working with James has been an excellent experience, and has served my business very well.

I have worked with other attorneys in the past, but now, Mr. Blake is the only one I call on for all my needs.

reviews.text: Micky is awesome.  She has a sharp legal mind and has a wonderful way of educating her clients about potential legal issues and more importantly providing options to help minimize or eliminate these potential problems.   Very warm and welcoming demeanor.

Working with Micky has been an absolute gift for our family and businesses. As estate planners go, she is truly exceptional. With over 15 doors that we own and manage through both our personal portfolio and our property management company, there are a lot of moving parts—and Micky caught everything. She guided us through the process of building a comprehensive Will and Trust that not only protects us today but also secures our family’s future and the financial legacy we want to pass on to our grandchildren.

Her attention to detail, professionalism, and genuine care gave us confidence and peace of mind every step of the way. If you are looking for someone who will go above and beyond to ensure your estate is in order and your loved ones are protected, I cannot recommend Micky highly enough.

I also want to say that I highly recommend her and her company—getting to know her through BNI has only reinforced what a top-notch professional she is. If you’re looking for someone who brings integrity, passion, and excellence to the table, she’s the one to call.

And just a little nudge—it’s about that time of year again for your annual trimming and tree care! Always happy to help keep those trees in their best shape. 🌳

Micky is an incredible attorney who really values her clients as individuals. When you talk to her you get a sense that she truly cares about you and your needs. I highly recommend meeting with her for all of your estate needs.

Micky is hands down the best estate attorney I have ever met. She actually takes the time to understand your needs and guide you towards the right solution. Her process makes you feel comfortable and stress-free and I would 10/10 recommend her to anybody who needs any estate planning help.

reviews.text:
Please think very carefully before choosing Farah as your lawyer.

Our experience was extremely disappointing and financially draining. We paid over $17,000 in legal fees for a divorce case and did not see meaningful progress or a final resolution. After such a significant investment, we expected clear communication, guidance, and strong representation. Instead, we were left feeling unsupported and without closure.

Communication became inconsistent, and many emails went unanswered. At a very stressful and emotional time, we felt left in the dark about the status of our case. Rather than helping us reach a proper conclusion, she has indicated she is withdrawing from representation, leaving the matter unresolved.

This process felt far more focused on billing than on achieving results. I strongly urge anyone considering hiring Farah to thoroughly research and ask detailed questions before making that decision.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Thank you for your review. Of course, we are very concerned whenever we see that a client has been dissatisfied with our services. First, even though this review was submitted in English, this client is not fluent in English. The language barrier was pointed out to the client in advance of retaining Ms. Ahmed. Nevertheless, the client insisted upon having Ms. Ahmed represent her. The language barrier required that each communication directly with the client, and all preparation for hearings as well as the hearings themselves, had to involve an interpreter and a third-party advocate. This obviously delayed communications and opened the door to miscommunications. In addition, the client often did not communicate, but rather had various family members and a third party communicate on her behalf. Because we are bound by client confidentiality, Ms. Ahmed could not respond to the family members or the client’s third-party advocate.

Despite the client’s claim of unclear communication, weak guidance and weak representation, she received very favorable outcomes in two temporary orders hearings, one providing her with custody, child support, financial maintenance, and the other order attorney’s fees to be paid by her opposing party. These positive results are clearly indicative of the quality of our representation.

As to the amount paid by the client, our documented records indicate $12,000, not the “over $17,000” claimed by the client. When Ms. Ahmed asked for the retainer to be replenished (as provided for in our contract signed by the client at the onset of representation) the client indicated that she did not have the funds. Yet she actually asked Ms. Ahmed to continue to represent her. Obviously, we cannot continue representation without compensation.

Subsequently, we were advised by the State Bar of Texas that a grievance was filed with the Texas State Bar. That grievance was promptly dismissed as not finding any professional misconduct.

The positive results at the two hearings clearly indicate Ms. Ahmed performed successfully. In addition to her complaint, this client and/or her friends and family are apparently engaging in an apparent attempt to manipulate our ratings by submitting a total of seven one-star reviews with inaccurate negative observations. Six of these reviews clearly violate Google’s strict rules that reviews may only be submitted by the client/customer. We are taking appropriate actions to address these fraudulent reviews.

reviews.text:
We hired this attorney to represent us in a divorce case and paid over $17,000 in legal fees. Unfortunately, despite the significant amount spent, we did not receive the progress or resolution we expected. Our case remains without a complete conclusion, and we feel we were left without clear direction or closure.

Communication became a major issue. Emails often went unanswered, and we were not kept adequately informed about the status of our case. At this point, she has indicated she is withdrawing from counsel, yet there has been no clear transition or formal resolution, which has left us feeling confused and unsupported.

From our experience, the process felt heavily focused on billing rather than achieving meaningful results. We understand that legal matters are complex and outcomes cannot be guaranteed, but we expected stronger advocacy, clearer communication, and a more definitive strategy after such a substantial financial investment.

We hope this feedback encourages improved communication and transparency for future clients.

reviews.text: It's been 10 years since the last time I chose Mrs. Brown to represent me. I still have problems with my ex wife asking for more child support. Mrs. Brown made me lose my 401k, my property, and never did anything to help me win anything is court. My ex doesn't even let me see my children for 6 years now. After I lost everything in court, Mrs. Brown and my ex wives lawyer went out for brunch. I would have let it go, yet I'm still haunted by all the damage that was done to me. Now I have a respirator and take medication for my heart. I was young and she knew this. No person should ever suffer like this. I won't expose my name, but I know that Mrs. Brown will remember this case. I'm not a phony person like you mentioned on your reply.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Thank you for your review, Mr. X. Of course, we are unable to provide a specific response for two reasons: First, you chose to use a pseudonym so we cannot research it. Secondly, we have handled hundreds of family law cases over the past ten years and it is impossible for us to remember every one. Having said that, we offer the following:
• We cannot recall even a single case handled by our firm in which one of our clients lost everything. Judges are reluctant to order dramatically lopsided property divisions, let alone award everything to one party and nothing to the other.
• We are very sorry Mr. X’s wife has not let him see his children for six years. But anything that happens after a divorce is adjudicated by a judge is final and is beyond our control. Mr. X would almost certainly have been granted visitation under all but the most egregious circumstances. Mr. X could have refiled a case against his ex-wife if she denied him visitation.
• The condition of Mr. X’s health is, of course, very distressing. We hope and pray he obtains quality medical care to address his health issues

reviews.text: A couple years ago 2022( time frame here is important!) I hired Farrah to help us gain full custody and adoption with parental rights taken from biological father. She did that. We were grateful. We asked how we were supposed to get the birth certificate she said I had to contact county clerk for the paper work and that’s all the information we were given. We got the paper work and the money and we went to the place to try to get the new birth certificate. They told us our attorney never filled the necessary paperwork and now we either needed our attorney to do what they were supposed to do or now we would have to do it all ourselves. We call Farrah and she tells us that this isn’t their job and it’s on us to go back to the county clerk and figure it out. Mind you she knew this and didn’t tell us anything about this. You would think after payments of thousands of dollars she would have been able to help us. Well now we are still stressed trying to figure out the birth certificate for health insurance purposes and my son would like to be home schooled but they need his new birth certificate to enroll him. We are still paying on the loan we had to take out to pay for her services to be abandoned with no information. More than likely we will get some sort of response about how it’s not their fault as I see another review has received. They get the job done ish. She got us through the court process but when I tell you she legit charges you if you speak for less than 5 minutes but yet can’t spend the few minutes to file the paperwork that the people in charge told me she was responsible for filing. Even after we went to court the last time I received another bill for god knows what I wish I remembered. But they will bill you for even more money and not tell you the job wasn’t completed. Just make sure you know exactly what you need done and what of it they will actually do. It’s a LOT of money to still be dealing with this years later.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Dear Ms. Buxton,,

Thank you for sharing your experience with us. We truly appreciate your feedback and understand how frustrating and stressful this situation has been for you and your family. Our goal is always to provide thorough and supportive legal services, and we regret that you feel we fell short in this aspect.

We are pleased that we were able to assist you in successfully completing the termination and adoption that you were seeking. Regarding the birth certificate, we sincerely apologize for any misunderstanding about the steps required following the adoption. Our records indicate that we provided guidance on how to complete the process independently, as discussed at the conclusion of your case. However, we understand that navigating these administrative steps can be challenging, and we would be happy to assist in clarifying any remaining concerns.

Please feel free to reach out to our office, and we will do our best to provide you with additional direction on obtaining the birth certificate. We value your trust and appreciate the opportunity to address your concerns.

Wishing you and your family all the best,

Patricia L. Brown

reviews.text: I have hired and worked with Farah Ahmad on my child support modification case. The video that they post about their firm they talk about having empathy which I yet to see. I have paid close to $20k and still haven’t gotten any child support on my case that was approved 7 months ago! I have requested my attorney for some order which she denied adding to my case stating “oh that’s not possible in Texas and that’s not how things are done here” I had my case prep in another state which was handled much better and professional. If you are looking to pay lots of money, have an attorney who calls you back while she is eating or late or in the evening then this is the firm for you.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Dear Ms. Reyhaneh,
Thank you for taking the time to share your feedback. While we wish all our clients were satisfied with our service, we certainly understand your frustration and sincerely apologize for any shortcomings you experienced during and after your case. We strive to provide compassionate and effective legal representation to all our clients. We value your feedback and will use it to improve our services.

Regarding your specific concerns:
We clearly understand your completely reasonable concern about obtaining timely child support. There are two phases to achieving that. First, to obtain favorable judgments from the court. Secondly, once you have a judgment from the court, to actually receive that support in a timely manner. Without going into any details of your case on this public forum in order to protect your confidential information, we believe that we performed every action necessary to obtain a very favorable judgment from the court in a timely and effective way, and did indeed obtain a ruling that was in accordance with your legal and financial objectives. We also completed the appropriate post-trial paperwork. This was the work for which you hired our firm, and we believe you were satisfied with this outcome. Once this work was done, our contract with you was completed.

It appears that your dissatisfaction results primarily from issues that have arisen after our work performed on your behalf was completed. We are no longer involved in this pursuit since you asked for direct contact with the office of the Attorney General. If you wish to re-engage us in this pursuit, please let us know.

As regards our communications with you, we acknowledge that communication is crucial in attorney-client relationships. In most family law cases, clients are eager to keep advised of any new details or changes in their case. We feel we represent our clients 24/7 and may be guilty of calling on weekends or evenings, if called for. We apologize for disturbing your evening. We will address this concern with Farah to improve communication protocols and more closely monitor individual client preferences in that regard.

Sincerely,

Patricia L. Brown

reviews.text:
As a father of one 17 years old boy and 15 years old girl, I wanted to get the custody of my children. You can image how hard it would be. First it was hard to find an attorney who would like to represent the case. Even if I found, they might just try to get more rights instead to get the custody. Thirdly, it would be very hard to win for my case. However, I found Patricia, who, represented me, tried to get what I pursued and won.
Patricia is an excellent attorney. When I first met her, she quickly caught the key points and understood what I pursued. She focused on clients’ need and was willing to try for me. After I interviewed 7 attorneys, I immediately hired her.
Patricia explained me all the procedures. She provided the guidelines for me to prepare the testimony.  She focused on material facts and nature.  She worked hard. Sometimes we had the conference call during the weekend.
Paula, the paralegal, is also very professional. She read all the documents I prepared. She read my Final Decree of Divorce and found the more violations of my ex, which I did not know before. And she acted quickly and efficiently and always answer my questions timely.
The fee is reasonable and may be saving.
reviews.ownerResponse: Dear Mr. Xiang, thank you so much for taking the time to comment both Patricia and Paula to help you secure custody of your children. We know how busy lives are these days.  And, yes, conference calls, etc. are often necessary as challenging family law issues don't take the weekend off. Thank you again, sir.

reviews.text: I retained Patricia and her team to get me through my divorce that had issues with child custody and substance abuse issues.  They were able to guide me through the entire divorce process and I ended up coming out the other side with exactly what I was seeking. It was important to me that tough, but fair, was the tone from my side through the entire process and Patricia executed that plan to a T. They were professional and good communicators and I'd recommend them for divorce proceedings to anyone.
reviews.ownerResponse: Dear Ms. Day, it was so kind of you to take the time to commend Patricia and her team for helping you get through your divorce. She knows personally and professionally how difficult divorce can be in so many, many ways. We wish you good luck and a happy and healthy future.

reviews.text: Mrs. Brown and her entire staff always work in a Professional Manner.  The quality of their service and dedication to their clients is exceptional.
reviews.ownerResponse: Dear Mr. Lucio, thank you very much for your kind review and very kind words. Nothing pleases us more than such client satisfaction. Thank you again.

reviews.text:
To say my case was a challenging one would be an enormous understatement.  Of all the child custody cases filed in the United States each year, one of the narrowest categories of victory in court is "father winning custody of special needs child from biological mother".  Patricia explained what needed to be done, laid out a plan of action, and coached /educated me every step of the way.  We won.  It wasn't easy, just like she said it wouldn't be...but in the end, my son will stay in the only home he's really ever known, most importantly, he'll stay with me.  Is Patricai Brown a good attorney?  Patricia Brown has become my good friend, who also happens to be the best attorney I've ever met.

My highest, 5-Star recommendation to Patricia, Paula and the entire staff at Patricia Brown & Associates.  You will not be disappointed in the results, and maybe equally as important...you will really like your attorney.

-Steve Fohl
reviews.ownerResponse: Dear Mr. Fohl, we cannot thank you enough for this exceptional review. We share your enjoy in helping you prevail in your challenging situation and winning custody of your special needs son. And to call out Ms. Brown not only as a good attorney but a friend is particularly meaningful. Thank you again, good luck and Godspeed to you and your family.

reviews.text:
Patricia L. Brown and Associates is an outstanding firm, providing exemplary legal representation.  Ms. Brown's knowledge and meticulous attention to detail are not to be understated in an area as crucial as family and divorce law.  She is very caring and understanding, and will always try to guide you to the best solution possible for you and your family, based on the situation at hand.

Ms. Brown provided legal representation in my child custody case.  I am not one to write public reviews, but felt this information needed to be shared.  I have worked with attorneys in various capacities for the past 15 years due to my profession, and I have not met another attorney with the same level of ethics, moral values, and overall concern for her client.  She navigated my complex situation gracefully, which allowed me to substantially increase time with my child.  As a father, I could not ask for anything better.  Heartfelt appreciation to Ms. Brown and her staff.

reviews.text:
I found Patricia through an online search when my ex-husband was filing for a divorce.  Once I met her and Paula, I knew I’d chosen the right firm.

Patricia provided guidance and support when I was going through a very emotional and difficult time.  She helped me understand all the steps in the process and what to expect with each one.  She and Paula were available to me by email and phone.  Instead of worrying about legal issues, I was able to focus on keeping my daughters and myself safe and healthy.

Since we first met, I’ve had to retain Patricia’s services when things come up with my ex-husband.  They understand the law around custody and divorce and all the little details that can easily scare someone in unfamiliar territory.

I believe her fees are reasonable and fair.  I would highly recommend her.

reviews.text:
Edited update after owner reply
Yes I had reached out to the Patricia Brown law office and many others regarding getting a CUSTODY modification order drafted, not divorce (don't even know what you are charging me for.) Upon discussing with Julia via email she had stated this
"That being said, our office would charge $500 for drafting of this Order. Let me know if you’re interested and I can send you a hyperlink and some minor paperwork to fill out that will give us all the details needed for entry of the Order."

Nowhere does it state; starting at, estimated to be, or about $500.. literally said we will charge $500 for drafting this order.. thanks for trying to CYA..

I had requested there office to draft an order for me.  They gave me a set price of $500 to draft the orde .  After going through procedures they had asked and obtaining documents needed to get the order drafted, I failed to hear from them for weeks, then when I finally was in contact, they doubled the price!! Not consistent, lack of communication, and not up front about pricin .
reviews.ownerResponse: We are sorry Mr. Walker was unhappy with our service. We view legitimate critiques as opportunities to improve client satisfaction.  In this situation, Mr. Walker and his spouse elected to do their own case before contacting us.  They appeared in front of Judge Betsy Lambeth in Williamson County who ordered the divorce. After this, Mr. Walker contacted us for the first time on February 7, requesting a cost estimate for drafting Judge Lambeth’s order. Having not seen the order, our paralegal replied drafting an order starts at $500 but ultimately depends on its complexity. She requested a copy of the court’s transcript. On March 2, Mr. Walker provided a copy of the transcript and, given its complexity, our paralegal told him it would be $1000.  With few exceptions, this is standard practice as no two family law situations are alike, and each must be individually assessed based on its unique complexity.  It is also noteworthy that at the end of the court transcript, Judge Lambeth said she would write the order and sent it to both parties! Finally, it is worth noting Mr. Walker never hired us and was not charged for our paralegal’s time.

reviews.text: Amazing attorneys! Extremely knowledgeable about every aspect of family law. They are never judgemental or make you feel as if they are not working in your best interest. They are so kind and helpful and go way above and beyond anything I ever expected.
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you for your lovely review. We appreciate it, Ms. McWonky 😉

reviews.text: Farah worked with me on my divorce and took care of everything! She took the stress off of me and was super knowledgeable. She was clear, direct and friendly, and would definitely recommend her.

Thank you for taking the time to leave such a nice commentary on Farah's efforts on your behalf. Relieving stress is one of our firm's critical goals, recognizing they anxiety divorce can cause. We wish you good luck and peace going forward.

reviews.text:
Made an appointment for today, upon arriving to the business park they are located in, I received a voicemail canceling the appointment for today. I noticed that the voicemail had only been recorded 30 minutes ago and asked to move to another time. So I got out of my car to walk in a reschedule, but the door was locked and no one was in the office, when I called to reschedule no one answered. My concern is that if this is how our business relationship starts, what can I expect of it in the future with something so important that I’m hiring a lawyer.

EDIT

Patricia contacted me regarding my experience and offered to make it up to me. She informed me that my appointment was an attempted to be rescheduled due to National Paralegal day, so her staff was out to lunch at the time and could not make it back to the office for my appointment.
reviews.ownerResponse: Mr. Sattler had a legitimate complaint. Because of a scheduling snafu, we belatedly tried to reschedule his appointment. This complaint has been shared with all our staff to help insure it does not reoccur. We apologized to Mr. Sattler and offered him a free consultation at his leisure. We are very sorry for this oversight.

reviews.text:
I have the worst regret ever for choosing Patricia L. Brown to be my Divorce Attorney. I keep finding her mistakes that hurt me financially and emotionally. I am spending more money to fix her mistakes than I could ever imagine nor can afford.
Overall, waste of hope, waste of time, GREAT waste of money!!
Whenever I have the time, and the knowledge, I will ask for a review on the Law Practiced and the bills charged by Patricia me to.
reviews.ownerResponse: We are very sorry Ms. Pires was unhappy with our service.  My paralegal and I worked very hard to try to obtain the results she was seeking. As with many of our cases, we pursued a mediated resolution, thus avoiding the expense and stress of litigation. When a mediated agreement is reached, we always caution clients to be very sure they are satisfied with the agreement before they sign it. Once it is signed by both parties, it cannot be changed. After over 20 years experience and more than a thousand divorce and family law clients, we have had some clients experience “buyers remorse” after signing such an agreement, so we take extraordinary pains to insure clients know what they are signing. Nevertheless, Ms. Pires was unhappy with the agreement and decided to hire another attorney to attempt to change it. We wish her all the best.
Thanks, Jeff. Five stars is 5 stars!!

reviews.text: Mrs. Brown was compassionate and super attentive. If I emailed her a question I would get an answer within moments. I highly recommend  her.
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you, Ms. Dayon, for taking the time to leave us such a lovely review. Two of the hallmarks of our firm are compassion and sensitivity to each individual clients circumstances, and responsiveness.

reviews.text:
I wouldn’t let my dog be represented by this challenged attorney. I’ve been waiting for paperwork for months and my attorney and myself have to continuously push her along to get anything completed. Everything is done at the last minute. I should have known this since she only recently passed the bar exam. Very little experience and when in her office, the phone isn’t ringing and everything’s quiet. This should tell you something. People that want a cheap attorney...use this one l...but you get what you pay for. Box of rocks!!

Mrs. Browns response should tell you everything. I’m still waiting on paperwork for an agreement made. Not upset about any type of verdict. It’s not hard to know where to start if you just do your job. Do your job and get me my paperwork. And do it right the first time. That’s where you start.
reviews.ownerResponse:
It is hard to know where to start.

First of all and most importantly, Mr. Miller was NOT our client. He was the opposing party: our client was Mr. Miller’s ex-wife. We have requested Google remove this review simply because it is not from one of our clients, which violates Google's rules, and it lowers our average rating. However, read on:.

Secondly, as to the allegations made by Mr. Miller, Ms. Brown passed the Texas bar exam 22 years ago, in 1997, which anyone could see in her biography on our website. Not only did she pass the bar 22 years ago, but she has been Board Certified in Family Law by the Texas Board of Legal Specialization since 2016. Less than 1% of all the lawyers in Texas are Board Certified in Family Law.

Thirdly, here is the review left by our client, Mr. Miller’s ex-wife, Deborah Sant’anna on our Facebook page:

I used Miss Patricia Brown and Julia for my divorce degree and child support modification. I couldn’t be happier. My daughter got everything that she was entitled to. We were able to do that before even going to court. Miss Patricia delivered everything that she promised for the price that we agreed before hand. I highly recommend her services.

No wonder Mr. Miller is unhappy. He is not the first opposing party we have made unhappy and he will not be the last.

reviews.text: Patricia is an excellent attorney. After firing a horrible attorney, we interviewed Patricia and decided to hire her. My daughter over the last year went through an exhausting year long custody battle with now her ex-husband. He did not like the judge's ruling and tried ridiculous things to get my daughter to move back in the same county and also get full custody of their son. Patricia is very caring and supportive, however she is also very intelligent and strategic. Her legal assistant Paula is also very good as well. She was able to implement very defensive moves and strategies with my daughter's ex-husband's ridiculous demands. We could not have gotten through this without her. She helped my daughter get through and our family get through a very trying time in our family. Our family had suffered the loss of my sister and Mom within 4 months of one another, and a heart attack three months later from my husband/her father. She truly cared about all that we had been through and made it so much better with her experience as a seasoned attorney. Thank you so much Patricia and Paula, God bless you for all that you have done. My daughter and our family can move on and start the healing process.
reviews.ownerResponse: Dear Ms. Foster, thank you for taking the time to write such a lovely and in-depth review. We are particularly pleased you also called out Paula, one of our two Family Law Board Certified paralegals. Sometimes, they are the unsung heroes of a strong law firm.

No lie on these attorneys reviews, I don't understand why they become attorneys if they are not going to help answer questions clients may have. I am pretty sure that they are not in court 24/7. Why even have a phone number listed if getting a hold of them is impossible.

There's no physical location, She doesn't return calls, emails, leaves you in a worse predicament with the court system if appointed....

She has been assigned to me in almost 1 years she has only returned 1 phone call I it has been almost 9 months I still can't get a hold of her, through email or phone decided yesterday to just call back to back to back.. today she started answering and hanging up on me...  Am fixing to go to the bar...
We have her court appointed and she never calls or answers her phone. She doesn't even deserve 1 star.
In the end she turned out to be a good lawyer. No complaints.

I called to ask about information on a case since the lawyer had not been in touch with her client and the person that answered the phone was so rude!  I could not believe how she addressed me over the phone.  Never in my life have I come across someone so impolite and rude.

My experience with this law firm was terrible.  I had been arrested for Obstruction of Justice/ Retaliation.  Something that should have only come out to public intoxication but my lawyer did neither for me Lacey A Mullowney looked at the technical side of the law and did nothing to argue, persuade, or move the court to a better decision she only agreed with the D.A. and allowed them to run rampant over me.  I would have done better on my own honestly she gave me ill advice, and is of ill repute in my eyes.  If you have the opportunity to have her as your lawyer do not.. However Cherie Ballard, her partner seemed astute willing to listen, and willing to fight for YOU, and not for the opposing side.  DO yourself justice and do not have Lacey A Mullowney represent you for anything.

I cannot comment on working with Evan himself as I didn't make it past the receptionist. I found Harlow Law Firm through my employer's Metlife providers listing for 'document review'. I only needed a contract document review before I sign it. When I called the reception, I was met with a curt, exasperated-sounding greeting when the phone was answered. I briefly explained my Metlife insurance and that I only needed a document review before being cut off by the receptionist who said "Evan only does estate planning!" reviews.ownerResponse:
Hello Kyle,
I am unsure why you have given me this review, especially considering my services for you were completed 18 months ago. I did "solve" your case and got you an agreed judgment that allowed you to pay off the debt over time with zero interest. I have even kept you informed of correspondence from the creditor in recent months as I received it. If your comment about "just collecting money" is in reference to my request to receive more attorney fees for the additional work, I would like to remind you that I (like yourself) don't work for free. My initial contract with you was long well and done and therefore any further work required further payment. If anything, with the amount of time spent on it I have worked on your case at a loss, and I challenge you to find an attorney who would have done the same work for the same price or less.

reviews.text: Richard Anton had to be fired and bought off my lawsuit just to go away and stop interfering for his own financial gain. While I was still in the ICU and heavily medicated, Richard visited my bedside and urged me to sign over a PIP check that had been paid to me, to him. He then kept all the money for himself, leaving me impoverished since I had lost the ability to work due to a motor vehicle accident I had while visiting Austin. Richard underestimated my ability to adjudicate against him from my homestate, Missouri, and he was surprised when everyone from the insurance adjustor to a Texas attorney took up cause against him for his wrongful practice. In the end I was not able to recover the PIP money he swindled from me, but I was able to buy him off my case. With another TX attorney who took my case on without charge, I was able to negotiated a larger settlement than Richard promised. This attorney is incompetent, crooked and solely self-interested. I was near death in an Austin hospital, where he practically forged a PIP check which had been paid to me. Don't trust him with any of your affairs.

I would give zero stars if I could. Anyone else would have been better than her.

Worst experience ever my mom past away, and my sister and I gave her 4,000 retainer to handle probate. After months have past she calls saying she  lost my mom's death certificate and needed another copy. SHE CHARGED ME TO COME TO MY HOUSE TO RECEIVED SOMETHING SHE LOST. After that she files paperwork and everyone name was wrong. She had a totally different last name for my mom, and someone I never heard of listed. After getting everything correct she tells me that I exhausted my funds and she needs another 4,000 before she can finish my case. I told her I don't have another 4,000 and that most of my retainer was spent on her redoing her mistakes. She tells me well pay me with the life insurance once you get it. She thought my mom was rich because I come from a middle and upper class family, and preyed upon my sister and I at vulnerable time. If I would had allowed her she would if just kept losing or messing up paperwork charging us for it until we had no more money left!!! Lastly every time we did speak she had worst attitude like we were bothering her, just sad!!!!
Awesome attorney!!!! She can get the job done!!

Ms. Webster is AMAZING!!! She is kind, sincere, driven and will do whatever it takes to help! I had been fighting for two of my children for 10+ years when I reached out to her. She helped me tremendously go up against a tough opponent and a really tough battle. Today I am a proud mother who is able to wake them up each morning and tuck them in each night. If it wasn't for her willingness to help I would be stuck in the same place I was before. She never gave up and had mine and my children's best interests at heart. I highly suggest her and her services. You will not be let down!!!!!!!

Say what you will about any attorney! But Ms. Webster opened her doors to myself and toddler son’s case when all the Greedy Williamson County/Burnett County Lawyers said no tell you pay thousands up front!
A+ in my book!

Excellent, kind and efficient. I highly recommend her. She was not only our excellent  legal counsel but also
our mentor and showed great humanity and kindness.

SHARON WEBSTER IS THE WORST ATTORNEY FOR FAMILY LAW, WASTE OF MONEY, NO RESULTS, ALL SHE WANTED WAS MORE $$$! DO NOT RETAIN!!!

This shameless known as Attorney Sharon Webster was hired to do a relocation trial between my ex-wife and I. She from the GET GO was demanding a $5k retainer. Long story short, she kept demanding more and more money ( In the end, I spent over $38k!!!) and she ended up LOSING my relocation trial and my ex got to move with my kid. She tried to put a different attorney on the case towards the end, and I felt that it was a very lazy move on her part, because she didn't know the case AT ALL!!! OH! To top it all off, I gave Sharon Webster a referral for my friends divorce and relocation and she forked over $35k!!!! She wasn't happy with the results either!!! The best part is, after the trial was over, she had some aggressive, threatening guy called me telling me "when are you going to pay your balance of $32k off??????? " SHARON WEBSTER STONE DIDN'T EVEN SEND ME A BILL, AND SHE HAD SOME GUY THREATENING ME, TELLING ME THAT SHE HAS CHECKED MY CREDIT AND SHE WILL GET EVERY DIME THAT IS OWED TO SHARON WEBSTER???? DISGUSTING!!!! Attorneys like Mike Sharon Webster Stone should get their license taken away. She is very greedy, and doesn't care about your situation, she just wants money. One time, I emailed her, and in her bill, she charged me $397.00 to REPLY TO MY EMAIL????????? NO thanks! Save your hard earned money and get an attorney that can be fair and passionate about your case. I am going to the BBB and the Texas State Bar about her behavior and professionalism. After MY $38+k, and my friends $35K, she still tried to charge me another $32k??? SAVE YOUR MONEY AND TIME, GET A LAWYER THAT WILL CARE AND NOT RIP YOU OFF!!!

WARNING DO NOT USE HER!!! WORST LAWYER EVER,  she charges me 3000.00 to help me with my divorce, she did NOTHING.. she never returns calls, doesn't remember anything about your case. Told me when the case was almost setteled that she was going to charge me another 3000.00 to finish it.. I ended up having to negotiate my own divorce with my x husband lawyer , who by the way said he was calling the barr on HER because she is sooo bad!! Don't use this horrible lawyer!!

Sharon helped me at a very difficult time and was nothing but professional, understanding, and extremely helpful.  I am indebted to her and her firm for their expert help guiding me through the legal process.

I contacted this Firm because they offer the service I needed. I would recommend Kevin to all my friends and family!! He was so helpful and down to earth. I felt like I’ve known him for years and that was so important to me. He was diligent, communicative and understanding!! He won my case for me and for that I’m beyond grateful.
Thank you Kevin for all of your hard work and going to bat for me. You took so much stress off of my shoulders. I really do appreciate you and all you have done for me.

Mr. Fostel’s reviews referencing his compassion, dedication and commitment to his prior clients held true throughout the two years that it took to finalize a settlement in my Personal Injury case due to a MVA that was not my fault. His concern for my health and recovery from my injuries was always at the forefront of our conversations. It was Mr. Fostel’s experience and knowledge of the Personal Injury Law, court procedures, and negotiation tactics that achieved a favorable settlement for my case.  I am incredibly grateful to Mr. Fostel and his firm for delivering in the way they did. I highly recommend Clay to represent anyone needing a Personal Injury Attorney.

Mr. Fostel recently represented me in a personal injury auto accident. He informed me of my options every step along the way while being extremely compassionate to the needs of myself and my family. He was persistent in getting my claim resolved.  I admire his great work ethic, his composure and outstanding communication.  I will add that him and his staff are always very professional. I would, without hesitation,  recommend him to anyone looking for excellence in an attorney.

I can’t thank Mr. Fostel enough for helping my get my car fixed after the dealership denied fixing it . Mr. Fostel was kind enough to reach out to me when I asked for help, and my family and I are so appreciative. I would highly recommend Mr. Fostel for anyone needing help with any car accidents or issues.

what I am reviewing here isn't any actual engagement - it never came to that - it is the lack of response. After an initial consultant visit where we agreed on 3 items I needed legal assistance with, I never heard back from the firm. I called back 2 weeks later, asking for an update and they told me I had not returned the 'Engagement forms' (or whatever they are called to get going). When I explained I never received such forms, the office clerk (not sure if that is the correct title, no disrespect) apologized and promised me a callback the next day to get it all sorted out. Now it's 4 weeks later and I am still waiting for the callback (well, not really, I found help elsewhere).
I was looking forward working with Sheehan because I totally see the benefit of what they claim on their website:
>>> Larger firms can be constantly tied up in countless cases — while you may be able to reach the front desk, it can be difficult to get into contact with the attorney who is actually representing you <<<
However, for me, the exact opposite was true.

I am very grateful for Attorney Farren Sheehan for guiding me through the probate process when my husband passed away unexpectedly.  Ms. Sheehan went the extra mile to assure peace of mind every step of the way.  Her availability, and most of all her professionalism, knowledge, generosity and kindness can’t be matched.  I also want to thank Ms. Sheehan’s professional office staff for their help and assistance in this matter.

Last year (2019) when I lost my mother, I hired Atty. Sheehan. She and her staff were thoughtful, professional, caring and supportive. Atty. Sheehan met with me and helped me navigate a very complex situation. She attended probate with me and guided me through the process. It was a very long road and emotionally draining for me. However, she and her staff helped to make the probate process transparent, affordable, accurate and less stressful. I have much appreciation to her and her staff for all of their hardwork and  I would recommend her services to anyone who hasn't gone through probate before.

Sheehaw law has been our family law firm for years.  Unexpectedly both of my parents passes away within 6 months last year and thankfully we were able to rely on this firm to get us through the complexity of probating two wills within months of each other.
Farren clearly explained the entire process to us and handled our court room proceedings with the utmost professionalism.  I also received excellent service from her office staff and paralegal.  I have recommended Sheehan law to three other friends and they have all been very pleased with the legal service they have received.

I called Sheehan Law with a vague idea of what I needed, but not quite sure. In our initial communication, any questions I had were quickly answered and it was clear that they knew exactly what I needed. The POA and DPOA were ready very quickly, and when we went in for signatures, they were also happy to notarize the HIPPA release that I brought along. The ladies in this office offer outstanding service and I would not hesitate to come back for any future legal needs.
We were in the rush to get a POA in-place for our adult child. Sheehan Law provided us thorough and timely service at an excellent price. Thank you!
Did my will and some POAs for my children. Super professional and very responsive to clients needs.

I was first introduced to Farren Sheehan, Atty, upon my mothers death and the need to settle her estate. I found her to be very sympathetic as we worked through the process. Since then, I have used her services to manage a trust from my father as well as assisting with drafting a promissory note and preparing personal wills for my wife and I. I have always found her to be extremely professional yet personable in all legal work she has done for us.

Great advice from this law firm. Upon completion of my Will and other documents,  it's all put together for you in am impressive binder.  I was also provided with a USB port so if I need to email anyone a document all I had to do was plug the port into my computer and send the document. I was very impressed with their service and would highly recommend for your estate planning!

When I initially filed, I was going in blind so I ran to a "billboard firm". This was an absolute mistake. After a whole year of spending thousands of dollars for literally no movement and cold-robotic "down to business" interactions where I felt like I was not being heard or supported, I jumped ship!
The Pennington Law Firm came highly recommended to me.

It was important to me I find an attorney who was queer-friendly and had experience with same-sex custody matters. I could not have found a more amazing attorney than Daniela Ibarra.

Daniela listened to my concerns, no matter how silly they may have seemed; was honest/blunt when I asked her to be; quick and efficient with her communication; and despite the process continuing to be lengthy, she never asked me to compromise for the sake of getting it over with (like the first attorney did). The absolute biggest thing to me though was that she understands the emotional toll a divorce involving a child can take on someone and ultimately what is "at stake". Not only did she fiercely pursue an outcome I can sleep with at night, she was extremely empathetic and supportive throughout the process.

The office staff is amazing as well! Shout out to Ruby!
Tyler Pennington, also a very excellent choice who has similar values and characteristics. Amazing crew over at Pennington Law Firm.

reviews.text: If you have a family court issue I recommend Tyler. The best word to describe him as a lawyer is logical. The best phrase “out beyond ideas of wrong doing and right doing there is a field, I’ll meet you there.” He is the type of Attorney that will keep you aligned with your initial agenda while keeping you planted. Today we mediated a 4 year long case. I absolutely never saw myself agreeing to what the opposition suggested and thought trial was the only path to victory. I was wrong. Thank you Tyler and team. May your field be full…..
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you so much for the kind words! So glad we could help!

reviews.text: I fully realize Tyler will respond saying he cannot respond to the details of the case, but it needs to be shared how negligent Jessica Houghtby was during our hearing and how Tyler Pennington would not own up to any of this negligence and mismanagement. Daniela Ibarra was our attorney initially, but she had a medical emergency that deemed her unavailable for an unknown amount of time.  Jessi stepped in with our understanding that she would be adequately brought up to speed on all the details of our family law case. Sadly, she came into the court room, late, rushed, and was writing down questions just before the judge entered the room. It was blatantly obvious how unprepared she was. There was no preparation whatsoever is terms of questions that would be asked, strategy, etc. I didn’t even know the format of the hearing until I was there. After speaking with a new attorney, I’ve learned how problematic and irresponsible this actually was/is. This lack of preparedness has deeply impacted our family, not to mention the thousands of dollars spent, heartache, and children that cannot comprehend this outcome. Tyler and Daniela told us throughout this entire process that we were good, our case was strong, we just need to get in front of the judge. It quickly turned to what we can do next (in other words, how can they keep billing us for more legal work). I write this in hopes that it’ll save others from this outcome, to at least ask all the right questions.
reviews.ownerResponse: As indicated in this review, due to rules of confidentiality we are unable to fully respond to the review in detail. I will say that we as a firm value our clients and their input. Additionally, family law cases are very fact and case specific and we never make any guarantees in our discussions with clients and in fact our contracts specifically mention that there are no guarantees. We as a firm handled this situation the best we could under the circumstances and I stand by our decisions. We attempted to remedy the concerns and feel that we addressed this the best way we could. Overall our clients are very pleased with the work we do and we don't profess to make everyone happy 100% of the time, as that is impossible in a business such as this.

reviews.text: Did not file quadro and lost $15,000 because of it
reviews.ownerResponse: We appreciate the feedback. While we can't comment on the specifics of this complaint in a public forum due to confidentiality just know that we are looking into this and we pride ourselves on high quality representation for our clients.

reviews.text:
Jessica decided to quit practicing law in the middle of my case in order to spend more time with her family.. She gave me 24 hours notice to decide if I would like Tyler to be my replacement attorney. Then about 2 weeks went by and I couldn't get anyone to answer an email or return a phone call. When I spoke with Taylor from their office on the phone she told me Jessica might have strep throat and wasn't able to answer emails but didn't offer any reason for Tyler not to be in contact.
I had to find a new lawyer. But Pennington kept half my retainer.
reviews.ownerResponse: We apologize you didn’t have the experience you expected. reviews.text:
I spent a lot of time thinking about how I was going to write this a couple of weeks ago. My review would have echoed what many have already said here. And that is, while it took a while for our case to make its way through the process, our attorney was by our side the entire time. Handling a custody case during the height of covid was no easy task. It involved many emails, phone calls etc. That being said, we readied for our first hearing with the judge and Ms. Ibarra knocked it clear out of the park. I mean, she brought the lumber. Whatever cliche you can think of for the utmost professionalism, client care, and knowledge of the law, she exceeds. As negotiations regarding visitation went ultimately to a mediator, Daniella clearly outclassed her opposing counsel by knowing the laws in Texas, offering reasonable counteroffers, and then clarifying the order until it was signed by the presiding Judge.

But here's where it gets even better. After the order was signed, we received a copy of the final order and proceeded on as if that would be the prevailing document. Only to have opposing counsel file a motion afterwards to change custody. As a non-lawyer I immediatley googled what the motion meant because we had already closed out our account with Daniella. Shortly thereafter, she returned our call and was willing to take this case back up on our behalf. Let me say that again, she had already closed this out and returned our retainer. She was under zero obligation to do anything. But she did.

While I hope to never need a lawyer regularly if I did or someone I know did, I would not hesitate to call Pennington Law. If you think I sound too good to be true, message me, I'll tell it to you the same way.
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you! We truly appreciate it!

reviews.text:
Mr. Pennington is hands down the best attorney I have had the honor to work with on my case. He is detail oriented, focused, and not afraid to be aggressive when needed.

To say he is knowledgeable is an understatement, he knows the law well and is able to explain it to the layman. He is wise enough to know when measured action is the best route. I was happy to take his lead, and felt he was attentive and responsive to my requests. I appreciate his ability to actually take in all the detail of a case (which in my case there was a lot), while keeping the big picture in mind. He was able to set a goal and did everything in his power to reach it, all while keeping me informed.

He and his staff are very responsive, personable, and compassionate people. He was worth every dollar, especially when I had spent over 40k in a year with my first attorney with little result beyond having a figure in court.

Mr. Pennington is also very well known in the legal and professional community as being a hard working, good hearted, ethical human. Judges in family law know him and had good things to say about him. Everyone from our Gaurdian Ad Litem, to our child therapist, to our supervised exchange service, had nothing but glowing words for Mr. Pennington. He is known for fighting for the cases he believes in and for fighting for what is right.

I found him through connecting with several other attorneys before him. He was referred to me by 2 other colleagues as someone who would be good for my case. My case being one that was unusually involved, was underrepresented in the past, and had significant CPS involvement. He is able to quickly see through the BS and can quickly identify cluster B personality types. For a single mom dealing with a manipulative and abusive ex with a young child in the midst - he was a godsend. I recommend him to anyone needing quality representation in family law.
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you so much! We appreciate you!

reviews.text: I highly recommend Pennington.

Excellent Law Firm... Served our family well since 2018... Thank you for your impeccable service!

Truly the Best Attorney We’ve Ever Worked With

Working with Mr. Pitts has been an absolute blessing to our family. He helped us through two major legal matters one involving a product that was not being properly handled under warranty, and another involving the removal of a lien and lawsuit against our home. Both situations were stressful, complex, and emotionally draining, but Mr. Pitts handled them with the utmost professionalism, care, and integrity.

From the very beginning, he was kind, respectful, and always on top of things. He communicated clearly, explained everything in a way that made sense, and consistently fought for what was fair and right. He was honest and straightforward, but never harsh he guided us with both wisdom and compassion. In a world where it can be hard to find people who truly care and do what they say they will, Mr. Pitts stands out. He is rare.

We are a family of faith, and we genuinely believe that God sent him into our lives during a very difficult season. He was not just our attorney he was a key part of helping us move forward, get things in order, and find peace again.

If you’re looking for someone who is not only an excellent attorney but also a person of great character, look no further. Mr. Pitts is everything you hope to find in legal representation: trustworthy, responsive, knowledgeable, and truly on your side.

We are forever grateful.

My husband and I used Tony Pitts for estate planning and to file our wills. Mr. Pitts was prompt, responsive and very courteous.  I appreciated the way he explained all the procedures and options. I would highly recommend him for estate planning.

This guy did a partian action case regarding my mom residence and the power of attorney that was done in his office with my mother that was deemed incompetent by her Dr because of her Alzheimer’s. She signed legal paperwork after the Dr’s report. I would by no way ever used this attorney, I also turned him into the state bar and the Secretary of Texas in regards to him using his notary for these documents.

$400 for 30 minute consultation. What a joke.
The lady who called could not answer my questions on what exactly was needed. She went straight into the cost of a consultation.
reviews.ownerResponse: You posted this review for the wrong law firm.  I answer my own calls and I don’t charge $400 for consultations.

reviews.text: I used Tony several times over the past few years and he has been excellent. His fees are reasonable and about right for legal services. He has helped me several times and each time came out with a posituve result. Great lawyer, nice guy. I recommend Tony Pitts.

I called Mr. Pitts, and we breifly talked about what was going on.  he suggested it would be eaiser to meet in person, once I recieved all the docementation.  When I called back staying I had it all.  Tony, easily scheduled an appointment which worked for both of us.
When I went into his office, he was very genuine, kind, considerate of my feeling.  He asnwered all my quesions, made me feel like I wasn't going to fail in life.  He then gave me an order to what I had no order in.  We finished, he said I could call him if I needed him again, so far I haven't had to call back.  But if I need another lawyer, I would call Tony!

I contacted Tony to request a consultation on modifying an existing lease, since I'm a first-time home buyer who's taking over property with tenants. I just wanted to remove a few of the stipulations that seemed unreasonable and make sure the lease agreement wasn't unlawful in the state of Texas, since I'm not familiar with Texas laws at all (never lived there before).

Tony took a full 6 days to get back to me, and tells me that he has a flat fee of $2,000 for custom lease agreements. How utterly disgraceful for a person to take advantage of someone who's just starting out, and trying to make a life for themselves. And shame on him for taking so long to reply. I'll be taking my business elsewhere, and I recommend you do the same. This guy is only interested in exploiting you for as much money as he can, and isn't even remotely interested in doing a good job.

Only reason I'm giving two stars is because he didn't lie about his services. His honesty about how much he's trying to put you through the wringer, and extract as much out of you as he can is about the only good thing about my exchange with Tony.
reviews.ownerResponse: I fail to understand how you feel justified in leaving me such a scathing and judgmental review when we have never spoken to or worked with one another.  Indeed, the number of words in your review outnumber those in our written exchange surrounding your inquiry.  You do not mention that the first thing I did in my message to you was to apologize for the delay in responding to you and explain that I had been busy with a trial.  You never asked me if the fee I quoted you could be negotiated based on your need. And if you did not like the fee I quoted you to build a custom lease, you could have said no thank you and gone somewhere else, but to accuse me of exploiting people with the intent not to even do a good job for them is uncalled for.  I have worked with over 1,000 clients that would readily disagree with what you have written here.

I was diagnosed with stage 4 cancer and was overwhelmed with chemotherapy and radiation treatments. I didn’t know I could be eligible for disability insurance. That’s when Bemis Roach  and Reed came to my rescue. I highly recommend this firm for their rapid response, efficiency and professionalism. But even more importantly, staff, like Amber,
really care about you. They have heart. They walk you through each step, assist you with all the paperwork and provide critical information that  makes the application process easier. With the firm’s help, I was deemed disabled and got my checks in about 5 months. The process can take well over a year so I was fortunate. I will definitely refer my friends and family to Bemis. This firm delivers results.

reviews.text:
It took me about four years to get through the disability process. The first time I tried on my own and was denied. After doing some research online, I came across Bemis, Roach & Reed through a Google search and decided to reach out. From the very first phone call, everyone I spoke with was kind, patient, and genuinely caring. They never made me feel like my situation wasn’t important or that I was just another case.
Amber Wooten was my case worker, and she was amazing. Over the years, she kept in touch, answered all my questions, and helped me stay on top of every piece of paperwork. She always kept my medical needs in mind and made sure things were practical and not overwhelming.
The office was great about communication — if I emailed or called, I’d always hear back the same day or within 24 hours. When it came time for my hearing, Mr. Bemis was incredibly kind and reassuring. His calm, genuine approach helped me feel more at ease during a really stressful time, and thanks to his guidance and the team’s dedication, my case was approved.
Everyone at Bemis, Roach & Reed treated me with care and respect from start to finish. I’m so grateful for their patience, understanding, and commitment throughout this long journey. I highly recommend them to anyone who needs compassionate help with their disability case.
reviews.ownerResponse: Wow, what a kind and thoughtful review ImRebeka! Amber, Lloyd and everyone at the firm expressed their appreciation of your comments. We are constantly amazed by the courage and patience of the people we get to work for as they go through the disability appeals process. We have the very best clients.

reviews.text:
I recently worked with this firm on my Social Security Disability (SSDI) case after years of struggling on my own, and I cannot express how grateful I am for their support. Every person I interacted with was courteous, professional, and genuinely helpful throughout the entire process.

My attorney, Mr. Messenger, was outstanding. He truly listened, took the time to understand my situation, and guided me step-by-step. He never rushed our conversations — in fact, he routinely spent close to an hour on calls to walk me through everything and make sure I felt confident and informed. His preparation for my hearing was thorough and thoughtful.

Because of his dedication and the firm’s support, my case was approved fully favorable with no issues. After everything I’ve been through, that outcome meant the world to me.

I strongly recommend this law firm to anyone needing help with a disability claim — whether it’s SSA or VA-related. They know what they’re doing, and they genuinely care.
reviews.ownerResponse: Daniel and the entire firm are so glad we could help Kino. Thank you so much for posting your reviews for us, it absolutely helps our firm, and we truly appreciate it. Part of the joy of our job is that we get to work with so many nice people. Thank you Kino.

We are so happy to have come across and engaged the services of Greg L. Reed, attorney with Bemis, Roach and Reed in Austin, TX. When a multitude of health issues made my wife unable to continue working, we knew we’d need help to navigate the complexities of filing for disability benefits with Texas Income Protection Plan (TIPP). We subsequently learned she’d also need to apply for Social Security Disability Insurance (SSDI) benefits. Both processes were lengthy, complex, arduous and required multiple levels of appeals to ultimately be awarded the benefits to which we believed she was rightfully entitled based on her adverse health developments. Fortunately, Greg Reed was just what we needed. Greg was kind, caring and encouraging, but also experienced with such complex cases, had a great ability to understand, remember(!) and convey her medical condition and associated jargon, as well as the legal minutiae necessary to ultimately prevail in our case. Greg’s accessibility, prompt, accurate and insightful communications, combined with his kind and patient demeanor made him a pleasure to work with. Greg also offered multiple payment options which enabled us to afford his quality representation. We believed her claim for disability benefits was warranted, but would certainly have been rejected if we didn’t have excellent legal representation. We couldn’t have done it without Greg Reed and the resources of Bemis, Roach and Reed! We have nothing but praise for our experience with Greg L. Reed - no complaints at all.
