I cannot recommend this firm enough. I recently needed legal representation for a civil case and worked with Mr. Ilionsky, and the experience exceeded every expectation I had.

From start to finish, he was incredibly professional, knowledgeable, and responsive. He clearly knew exactly what he was doing and guided me through the entire process with confidence and clarity. What could have been a stressful and overwhelming situation ended up being smooth and manageable because of his expertise.

He saved my husband and I an incredible amount of time, energy, and money, and always made himself available to answer questions and provide updates. I truly felt supported and well-represented every step of the way.

If you are looking for a civil attorney (or an attorney in general), who is prompt, skilled, and genuinely committed to helping their clients, I highly recommend The Ilionsky Law Firm. I will absolutely be referring friends, family, and anyone in need of legal services to them in the future.

Thank you again for such outstanding representation!

After a long search for the right attorney, and after wasting nearly a year with a previous lawyer who didn’t move my case forward, I found EZ. From our very first phone call, I knew things were different. He returned my voicemail right away and took the time to really listen to my situation.

At the time, I hadn’t seen my three-year-old daughter in over a year after she was moved out of state. I was just trying to get visitation and reconnect with her. EZ was clear, honest, and confident about what we could do. He immediately got the ball rolling and fixed issues left behind by my previous attorney.

The difference was night and day. EZ kept me in the loop, prepared me for court, and truly fought for me. Despite constant pushback, he stayed steady and got us into court quickly. Because of his work, I was granted step-up visitation, then unsupervised time, and eventually we reached mediation where I was awarded a Standard Possession Order.

He even helped prevent retroactive child support that they were aggressively pursuing. More than anything, though, I felt like he genuinely cared about me being a father and rebuilding my relationship with my daughter.

Every case is different, but in mine, I truly couldn’t have asked for a better attorney. Thanks to EZ, I’m now preparing to have my daughter spend her first full week with me in Texas for Spring Break…something I wasn’t sure would ever happen.

The second time we retained Ilionsky Law was in early 2024 to assist my mother through a very difficult civil matter with said family member. Kathyrn and Donna were very thorough and genuinely thoughtful while they took care of me.  Kathryn’s approach was brutally truthful and honest but real and protective.  She really was the best representative  for me during my separation process.  She not only offered legal advice but also personal care and concern.  I would hire her again in a heartbeat!  Thank you for giving me strength and courage!

Kathryn is the best family law attorney there is! She helped me with my child custody case a few years ago. She is very professional, experienced, "down to earth", and gives great advice. Kathryn and DeAnna (her assistant) are very quick to respond to messages and keep you well informed throughout the process. Kathryn even went above and beyond to fix an issue that arose in my case 2 years later. I would definitely recommend her for any of your family law/attorney needs.

I am so fortunate to have found Kathryn, she and Deanna have made my children and I a top priority from the beginning. I wanted to insure that everything was done right and my children would be taken care of after my divorce. Please listen to your attorneys, you hired them to look out for your best interest and Kathryn and Deanna did just that for me. The legal system is confusing to navigate without professional help. Every form was made clear to me and all options and outcomes were thoroughly explained so I would be prepared for future arrangements. I was able to put my feelings aside and make choices that best suited my family thanks to Kathryn’s direct approach. You ladies Rock!!! Thank You and Thank You again.

I am so glad I found Kathryn Figueredo Fowler. She was prompt and very professional and got me exactly what I was going for. I highly recommend her if you feel like you are going into a one sided battle against anybody.

I used Katherine for a 1 year long divorce and I would highly recommend her. She is very patient and actually cares for you and the case. Her assistant Deanna is also amazing - you are actually a person with them and not just money coming in. HIGHLY, HIGHLY recommend the both of them!

If I could give a Negative rating, I would! During my phone consultation (which I expected to be private), I soon realized I was on speaker phone, and I could clearly hear people talking in the background which was exposing my privacy.

I had asked a few questions for Fowler and told her a few things about my case. She began judging me right away. She was very abrupt, and she even mentioned of what she heard so far about my situation, she was rude in telling me to just to move to Texas (since I am living in another state) and why would I be stupid enough (And Yes, she called me Stupid!" to be living far away from my child. She continued to talk over me, and interrupt me while I was explaining my case. After hearing her being more judgmental and raising her voice, she then rudely hung up on me! (The circumstance of me living in another state, is that my ex is in the military and moves around).
I would NEVER recommend this individual. She is unprofessional, arrogant, and egotistic!
BEWARE, if you hire her, you would be wasting your time and your money!

My divorce took 2.5 years, I went through 4 lawyers before and was grateful that I found Ms. Fowler. She finalized the divorce for me in 2012 and I wish I went to her first.

Just last year my ex dragged me to court again because of some disagreement. We almost reached an agreement without court hearing but Ms. Fowler insisted on not agreeing with two of the requests. I do not know how she preps for court but she was professional, smart, asking point-blank questions and established that my ex had no case. She did so well in court that we won completely. My ex would have been better off if settled. I was so glad I followed her advise.

When it comes to lawyers, they are not all at the same level- I have a pile of expensive bills to prove it as I had hired and fired quite a few. In the end, Ms. Fowler is someone you can trust to fight a good fight for you, to give you the best advise for your situation and to follow through with a solution.

Thank you Ms. Fowler!

Wouldn’t recommend at all. Doesn’t itemize your statements properly or at all. Did not communicate well, and honestly took me for more money then she was worth. She told me I’d have to give money back to my child’s father if I didn’t move back to Texas. Felt she favored men over helping women. Don’t bother with her if you want true integrity.

Kathryn was able to see me quickly initially, gave me truthful and honest advice, and was compassionate during my divorce. She stood up for me when the divorce became difficult, and did not allow me to be intimidated by the other party. She went out of her way to help me with legal issues that came up as a result of my divorce as well as the divorce itself. I feel that she represented my interests well and was very pleasant to work with. If you are like me, and have had little to no prior experience with lawyers and aren’t sure who to trust, I would highly recommend Ms. Fowler. Her paralegal Ms. Cornelius is also very pleasant, and ensures that you know and understand everything that is going on. Overall I believe that I would use Ms. Fowler’s services again, and recommend her to anyone that desires a no nonsense, straightforward, and experienced attorney.

Kathryn handled my case very professionally and gave me very good advice.  Thanks to Kathryn I was able to overcome some tough legal challenges.  She was quick and very well prepared for my child custody fight.

reviews.text:
I am very grateful to Jeanie Zhang, the attorney, and Sixing Zha, the case manager, for their outstanding support in handling my immigration case.

Their professionalism, dedication, and attention to detail made the whole process much smoother and less stressful. They were always responsive, patient, and truly cared about helping me every step of the way.

I sincerely appreciate all their hard work and highly recommend their services to anyone in need of immigration assistance. Thank you both for your excellent support!

Not sure how this firm received such good reviews. They took my car accident personal injury case and then dropped it two months later, stating it was not worth pursuing. As a result, I was left responsible for all the medical treatment costs they directed me to obtain after accepting my case. Also the whole process they were not trying to help you but always evaluating how much money they can get from the case.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Thank you for taking the time to share your feedback. We’re sorry that you felt disappointed by your experience.

Personal injury cases are initially evaluated based on the information available at intake. As a case progresses, new facts—such as liability concerns, insurance coverage limitations, or medical causation issues—may arise that make it legally or ethically inappropriate for our firm to continue representation. When that occurs, we notify the client promptly so they may pursue other options.

We’d also like to clarify that we do not direct clients to incur medical bills for which they are personally responsible. During representation, treatment referrals are typically made under a Letter of Protection, meaning clients are not billed upfront. Any medical expenses a client remains responsible for generally involve treatment obtained before representation began (such as emergency care) or cases that cannot proceed due to liability or fault issues.

We wish you the best moving forward.

reviews.text: My father has terminal cancer and only three months to live. He wants me to become a U.S. citizen. I'm too busy with work and have limited time to study, so I've failed the citizenship test multiple times. This time, I decided to hire a lawyer because I didn't want to take the test again or try the new version. A friend recommended Attorney Zhang, and I only passed the English version today thanks to her. Attorney Zhang's responsiveness is top-notch, and I'm also grateful to my examiner. Friends taking the citizenship test, please don't fill out the forms yourself! A single mistake could lead to serious consequences. I almost passed this time, but then I had to ask a friend to fill out the form and I had to submit additional documents – I regret it so much!

I had an excellent experience working with attorney Jeanie Zhang and case manager Sixing Zha on my immigration case. From the day we submitted the application to the day I received my green card, the entire process took only six months, which exceeded my expectations.

1）From the very beginning, the document checklist was clear, detailed, and easy to follow. I simply prepared my materials according to the list provided, which made the whole process much less stressful.
2)They also managed the overall timeline very well. At each stage, I followed the schedule and instructions given by the office, and everything moved forward smoothly and on time.
3)In the final stage, the interview preparation was especially helpful. The guidance focused on the key points and typical questions, so I felt confident going into the interview—and we succeeded on the first try.

I am very grateful for attorney Jeanie Zhang and case manager Sixing Zha professionalism, clear communication, and support throughout the entire process, and I would highly recommend them.
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you for choosing Springdale Law Group.

My brother is a diagnosed paranoid schizophrenic and gets a check from the government every month. In 2017 he was arrested for evading arrest, a symptom of his schizophrenia. Instead of fighting for his rights, she had the paperwork filed and sealed. It wasn't till recently that the Western District Court of Texas (Austin division) agreed with my brother after he filed a petition for writ of habeas corpus pursuant to 28 U.S.C 2254 (ECF No 1).
Really sad that people who trust on others to defend their rights, seem to be the ones nobody cares about.
Very efficient, thorough, knowledgeable and friendly. I'm glad she was able to assist.

reviews.text: No one ever expects to have to contact an attorney, but when you do; you want someone who is extremely qualified and trustworthy. reviews.text: I have been in a crisis mode for one year (started June 2015).  It started with a marital separation and has not stopped until this month, May 2016.  Jeremiah Williams and Dolores Ziegler have taken care of me, personally, and my legal needs.  What would I have done without them this year?  My guess is that I would have been terrified and inept without them.  They have been supportive of my unsure times and of my decisive times.  Jeremiah Williams is a compassionate man that has advised me and assisted me in many ways.  He shared himself with me so that I could attempt to feel more "normal" and less stressed.  And it was so.  Jeremiah is available to the phone and was on my initial contact call.  I was surprised and also comforted as my needs had gotten beyond my abilities to deal with.  I am 63 years old and have required an attorney a couple of times in my life.  I will be in contact with Jeremiah and Dolores for the duration of my remaining life because they took care of me like the emotional child that I was when I needed them.  In closing, a moment of humor, please.  My friend and ex-wife of recent proceedings has a nickname for Jeremiah that cracks me up.  I would share it with you in person, but not online.  Least it be said that to me his nickname is totally fitting to his active, very active, presence and agility around ones feelings and the court room and in every dealing I have seen him encounter.  The nickname is so clever.  I will tell Jeremiah, if I haven't already, and he can tell you if he wishes.  It's totally great if you're his client.  Best regards

reviews.text: Mr. Williams was highly professional and knowledgeable. He gave good advice, but I made foolish choices not to follow it. DO NOT HIRE THIS MAN OR HIS FIRM! We hired him to handle a child custody case for my husband & I. He did nothing that we asked. He is hard to get a hold of, and will make dates for a phone conference & then just never call. We were told that if he didn't contact us, we should call them. He never communicated what was happening. He made a matter that should have taken 2 months- 5 months.
We should have just throw the 4K out the window and that would have done us just as good! He isn't even finishing our case-by our choice & we now are having to hire another lawyer.
He did not work for us. He gave in to everything the other party asked. He didn't protect my husband or myself. We warned him of issues and he told us to "be adults and get over it". When those issues happened, he refused to acknowledge it. When we asked for legal advice, and he gave it (in the shortest terms possible). We followed said advice & then we were told BY him that we were in the wrong.

Save your money, hire a different attorney. But not him.

reviews.text: No stars if google would let me. I see so many good reviews and I wish I could say the same. I wish that he had fought for me and my childrens' welfare like he had promised to do in the first place. No, he split the case in 2 parts for more money when he did not have to.  He had wanted me to sign off my custody of my children to my abusive ex. He cared less for their welfare.  If I had known the law, I would have known I was entitled to get 50% of the money owed from the sale of my marital property. He cared less to get that for me as well....in whole, he did not even fight for my basic rights. It was like I was trash to him. I write that because after all the wrong that was done me in the court system by my ex and his lawyer,  it appears that he too believed their manipulating stories and started to treat me as such. I thought he was helping me at first by not charging me as much as he had normally would being I was a home mom,  but even then I was ripped off.  It is very sad. I really hope this is not a regular thing of him to take advantage hurting mothers or anyone that just wants justice. I believe he could have won for me even now, but he just did not care to do so.

reviews.ownerResponse:
Thank you for sharing your feedback. We are sincerely sorry to hear about your dissatisfaction and take your concerns seriously. At RRK Law, we are committed to providing the highest level of service and integrity in all our dealings. While we cannot comment on specific cases due to strict confidentiality obligations, we assure you that we always operate within the legal and ethical frameworks that govern our profession.
We understand that legal processes can be complex and sometimes outcomes may not align with the expectations of all parties involved. However, we want to reassure everyone that we diligently work to represent our clients' interests and strive for the best possible outcomes under the law.
We take allegations of misconduct very seriously. As such, we are currently pursuing appropriate legal channels to address and resolve this matter, including seeking a court order for the removal of content that we believe to be defamatory and not reflective of our firm's values and practices.
We appreciate the trust our clients place in us and remain dedicated to upholding the highest standards of the legal profession. For anyone seeking more information about our services and our firm's track record, we invite you to contact us directly or visit our website.
We thank you for your understanding and continued trust.

reviews.text:
I am writing to express my concerns regarding the service I have received recently. As a client of West Short & Howell since 2008, I have always valued the excellent legal support your firm has provided to both my personal and business matters.

However, I have experienced some challenges in contacting my attorney through email and phone. Despite assurances that my messages would be relayed, I have not received a direct response or call back. In addition, my experience during a recent office visit was not satisfactory. I found the behavior of Sara, the receptionist, to be unprofessional and discourteous. Her lack of greeting upon my arrival and her rude, aggressive, and defensive responses when I inquired about the communication issues were quite disappointing. I suggest that Sara could benefit from additional customer service training to enhance client interactions.

Considering these concerns, I have decided to seek legal services from another firm.
reviews.ownerResponse: The last time we worked with Max was in December 2023, and we are sorry to see our relationship break down like this, but we believe Max's comments are unfair, not true and believe he left out some important facts.  Max had emailed one of our associate attorneys directly about setting up a time to talk with that attorney, and then called and left one message for him (in March), which was relayed to the associate attorney.  He did not set up a time to meet with the associate, but instead came into our office angry and raised his voice at our Office Manager and at our Legal Assistant, claiming he had called "3 or 4 times" and had emailed the associate "at least twice," neither of which is accurate.  When our Office Manager tried to set up a meeting for him, and provided him with our updated term and conditions for our services, he said he was taking his business elsewhere.

reviews.text:
I sincerely hope others do not have to go through the experience I had with attorney Milissa Barrick at this firm. In my experience working with her, I did not feel adequately represented in court—documents and evidence I had provided that were key to my case were not presented because copies were not available at the hearing, and I even had to clarify information about my own case in front of the judge. I also observed the judge correcting statements made by Milissa Barrick multiple times, which was concerning to me as a client. After the hearing, I was told in a call by Milissa Barrick that the judgment was “just” and that I needed to “accept my mess ups,” which was very discouraging to hear from the attorney I had hired to advocate for me.In my experience, I often felt that my attorney was not fully advocating on my behalf and there were some SERIOUS trust issues.

I initially had a five thou trust, but within about two months the total charges exceeded over sixteen thousand. Despite requesting in writing that the firm withdraw from representing me after the hearing, I continued to be billed, which left me feeling trapped and frustrated. I chose this firm because they were among the least expensive options I found when calling around, but the costs ended up being similar to other firms. Based on my experience, I wish I had considered representing myself instead, as the outcome created significant challenges for me and my child that will take years to work through.

I can attest that other negative reviews I read reflect experiences similar to my own and I wish I had paid attention to them. My experience with this firm raised serious concerns about their billing and representation practices. I would not hire them again, and I strongly recommend carefully considering other options, asking around on social media, and exploring other firms before deciding. From my perspective, after being with nightmare of a firm, I realized that there were better options available out there as representation, I couldve even rehired one of my former lawyers. This was the worst experience of my life with the worst possible outcome.

Edit:It’s been 3 months since I had one of the worst experiences with this law office. My case has been closed for three months and I’m still seeing mystery charges on my account with no explanation. I’ve never experienced anything like this with a business. If the case is closed, why am I still being billed? I would strongly recommend not hiring them, it is frightening, you will feel trapped if you do.
reviews.ownerResponse: Hi, Ana. At Law Office of Bryan Fagan, PLLC, we aim to provide positive experiences at all times. Is there anything we could do to make it better? Please call us at (512) 882-3303!

reviews.text:
I’ve been working with Bryan Fagen’s law office for about four months now. Initially, I was optimistic, believing I had someone genuinely caring and committed to helping me. We had one meeting to discuss my issue. After about two months, they had drafted three different counter-petitions. The first one seemed reasonable, and I was okay with it. However, the second and third petitions were not disclosed to me. I had provided them with a USB drive containing all my text messages, receipts, and pay stubs.

A day before the hearing, I called my attorney, Melissa Barrick, to inquire about the information she would be presenting. She couldn’t provide a clear answer and seemed unaware of the details of my case. To make matters worse, she requested all the information I had provided months in advance, claiming the files were corrupted and needed to be re-uploaded.

On the day of the hearing, she presented incorrect information that I was unaware of, which severely compromised my case. To add insult to injury, she arrived in ripped pantyhose that she claimed were leggings, paired with a leopard print skirt that reached above her knees. It appeared as if she had just gotten out of bed.

While I don’t mind her attire, presentation is crucial, especially in a child custody/support case. Moreover, I was charged $11,000 for four months of minimal work. They charged me for emails that required only one-sentence responses and $275 for a 10-minute phone call for basic questions. Additionally, I was charged $950 to file papers.
reviews.ownerResponse: We sincerely apologize for your experience and appreciate your feedback. Your concerns regarding communication and case handling are taken seriously. Please contact our office directly so we can address this matter and work towards a resolution.

reviews.text:
**Updated to 1 star. 2nd review …This was a complete WASTE of money and the lawyers in my case missed answering questions I had on legal matters that had deadlines….. but they loved to charge for reading the emails. I sent emails constantly trying to get an answer, would call and no responses. PLEASE do not use this guys. I switched to another local lawyer who resolved my case within 2 months, and would communicate in a timely manner and had to undue a lot of this law firms errors. This las firm still haven’t even refunded my trust account money, even though I haven’t worked with them for over 2 months.

My 1st reviews- This has been a frustrating experience due to the amount of money I’ve spent to have VERY  LITTLE  communication from my team. I understand that lawyers cost and they bill for mostly anything, but I’m constantly billed from me asking a question, getting no response or legal advice, plan of action, etc. so, I re-ask, get billed from them seeing the email, and still  no response…. If I’m going to be charged for viewing emails, I would rather be charged that same amount for them saying they’ve received /viewed /discussed it at least.  The 2 times I’ve asked and have been responded in 2 months of working with this team, the time passed for when I needed the answer and had to figure it out on my own.

I’ve asked for a phone call, update on my case, called the team myself and get no responses in any form.

Overall, my thought process is if I’m going to be sent legal documentation or send information pertinent to my case, my legal team should be advising and guiding me on what they think and it just doesn’t happen.

Definitely look elsewhere if you want more communication, advice, answers , etc.

reviews.text: I hired her for an employment law matter, where I was discriminated against because of my race. She helped me through the entire EEOC process and always checked on my well being! I was surprised she was a lawyer because of how great her quality of work and qualifications were! We prevailed and won the victory against the employer!

I highly recommend working with Nicole Conger. Her tenacity coupled with her impressive experience left me feeling empowered and confident. She always made me feel heard and never rushed our time together. She was honest and practical while also positive. I interviewed many different firms and chose to work with Nicole because of the level of attention I knew she could give my case, and the determination she communicated through her words and actions was unmatched.

Nicole has been an absolute godsend to work with, my partner had been very anxious over his employment case before calling her, and since then a weight has been lifted. Her positivity is infectious but she is also going to be very real with you, which for me is a perfect combination of empathy and professionalism.

I didn’t have a good experience with Nicole. I’m sure if I ever need legal counsel in the future she might be a good fit, But for now my experience was short and unpleasant. I contacted her through email for legal representation based on her reviews, however she sent me a list of other lawyers to contact because she said she’s booked and is the only person in her firm along with dealing with a death situation with losing another client.
reviews.ownerResponse: To anyone reading this review, anyone else would have said thank you for the multiple referrals I sent to you! Your words are only from the evil in your heart, especially because I told you I am so sorry but I just lost a client and to please pardon me.

reviews.text: I've seen her location of her law firm and I've seen that she can definitely win any case that's on mind because it's her specialty to do so .

Nicole was exactly the kind of lawyer you hope for in a situation you never planned to be in.

From the start, she took the time to actually understand the context, the power dynamics, and what mattered to me. At every step, I felt informed.

Nicole consistently gave advice that balanced legal reality with practical outcomes. When it came time to negotiate, she was firm, thoughtful, and effective.

What stood out most is trust. I always knew where things stood, what the tradeoffs were, and why a particular approach made sense. That level of clarity and professionalism is rare.

If you need an attorney who is smart, steady under pressure, and genuinely on your side, Nicole is exactly who you want in your corner.

Nicole C. was professional and handled my case with care. While the process had its challenges, she ultimately advocated for me and got the job done. She is very kind and straight to the point. Love that about her! Thank you for all your help !

Hands down the best Attorney around! She was so kind, very informative, and fought for everything i asked for! She went above and beyond to make sure I was heard and understood every step of the process! I highly recommend her! Couldn't have asked for a better Attorney/Client relationship!

Nicole isn’t just a good lawyer, she’s a GREAT person.  I may have never gotten my case resolved had it not been for the dedication and commitment of Nicole.  I spoke with several lawyers early on and I was only interested in an attorney who truly loved the LORD God, Jesus Christ and not just used lip service without any commitment.

Well, Nicole was all about commitment.  She contacted me on a regular basis and kept me informed of the situation and the progress of my case.  We just resolved my case and that all started with Nicole believing in the conditions of my case and the idea that my former employer had violated my rights and terminated me for alleged violations.

I can never repay Nicole for all that she’s done for me but I’m more than happy to let you all KNOW that if Nicole can help you, that’s what she’s going to do.  She’s a great person who just happens to be an attorney and she’s more than ready to fight for you.
Nicole is a breathe of fresh air!  I am very impressed with her skill set and wonderful personality 🤗.
Nicole is awesome, If anyone i know ask me about get a Attorney. I will say Nicole Conger of Austin Texas. She responds fast.

I reached out to Nicole regarding a specific issue after being referred by a friend who had previously worked with her. While she let me know she was booked, the response I received consisted of a broad list of attorney referrals spanning multiple unrelated practice areas (employment, criminal, personal injury, defense), followed by a message indicating uncertainty about which attorneys might actually be appropriate for my issue. It started with “my heart breaks for your story” which at first felt very sincere but when she threw out all these recommendations it began to feel very copy paste.

I understand that solo practitioners have limited capacity, and I appreciate being told upfront when someone is unavailable. However, the lack of clarity and specificity in the referrals — especially for a very defined area of law — left me feeling that my issue hadn’t been fully understood or thoughtfully considered.

Based on this interaction, I decided to seek counsel elsewhere. I’m sure this firm may be a good fit for others, but this experience did not align with what I was looking for in initial intake or guidance.

Update: Soon after posting my original review, I was contacted directly by the attorney and asked to remove or change the review immediately. She explained that she was dealing with the death of a client two days ago but a previous review with similar language said a client died four plus weeks ago.

I explained that I would only consider updating my review after a future positive interaction and declined to make changes on demand before that point. The call became tense when I would not agree to do so. Being contacted and pressured regarding an honest review made me uncomfortable and reinforced my decision to seek counsel elsewhere.

At this time I cannot recommend her firm. I hope things change for her in a positive direction and wish her the best.
reviews.ownerResponse: First of all, the information in both reviews were total lies. Your first review words were that I knew someone who sent you to me. I called you twice to reach you when I got the review, and of course, I asked for you to remove the review, as no lawyer would not make that ask. You are not a client and your experience was not that of a client. Next, I asked you who the person was who sent you to me. When you said your friend's name, I had absolutely no idea who that person was. Hence, you lied from the start on your review. You said that you were getting ready and could not hear me, and I repeated three times, friend I lost a client three weeks ago. I said that I could fit you in for a call, in about two weeks. You then said you understood death but that I should actually consider the facts of your case. With no remorse and absolute entitlement, I will continue speaking to folks who are instant family, fill me with kindness, and that show me honor, and leave me positive reviews with truth, instead of trying to leave a bad review of entitlement. For the references out, I actually have people call in for a wide range of issues beyond employment who shout a praise of thanks for even contacting them back personally as the Owner and only Attorney and providing such helpful support.

reviews.text:
Working with Nicole Conger has been one of the most uplifting and empowering experiences of my life. From the very first conversation, Nicole made me feel heard, believed, and supported during a time when my world felt upside down. She didn’t just take my case,  she took on my fight as if it were her own.

Nicole’s compassion is unmatched, and her dedication is extraordinary. She communicates with clarity, honesty, and genuine care. Every message, every update, and every action she takes reminds me that I’m not alone and that someone truly brilliant is standing in my corner.

Her knowledge of employment law is incredible, but what truly sets her apart is her heart. Nicole brings warmth into a process that can feel cold. She brings confidence when doubt starts creeping in. She brings hope where others might see obstacles.

I’ve watched her handle my case with unwavering professionalism, strategic precision, and a calm strength that gives me peace of mind every single day. She never rushes, never pressures, and never makes me feel like just another file. I feel like a person she genuinely cares for, and that means everything.

Choosing Nicole as my attorney was one of the best decisions I’ve ever made. She helped restore my confidence, my dignity, and my belief that justice can be done. I’m truly grateful for her, not only as a lawyer, but as a remarkable human being.

I would recommend Nicole to anyone who needs an advocate who is smart, fearless, compassionate, and deeply committed to doing what’s right. She is simply the best.

Nicole is a person that makes you feel like family and will fight for your rights. She has been so easy to talk to in a horrible situation and I never felt judged always supported. You do need need to look further if you are looking for someone who is honest, has integrity and will always be available for questions or concerns. My case was settled in a short time and she did all the leg work. I am truly grateful for her professionalism and compassion along the way.
Thanks Nicole, you were professional and helpful on resources provided and directives for a resolution.
Nicole was professional, compassionate, and handled our case with dignity. I would recommend her to everyone I know.
This is by far the BEST law firm I have ever hired. I had the privilege to work with Nicole and she held my hand through the entire process. Thank you so much ❤️

Nicole Conger is the best attorney I’ve ever had. She helped my partner and me with a case that had been going on for over four years, and she handled it with so much care, professionalism, and confidence. Thanks to her, we finally have peace and closure. I can’t recommend her enough, she truly brought so much relief and joy back into our lives. 10/10!

Nicole is one of the most genuine and compassionate attorneys I’ve ever met. She truly listens and fights for you and what is right.  You can tell she cares deeply about what she does. If you’re facing discrimination or just need someone who will stand up for you with integrity and heart, I can’t recommend Nicole enough. She held me during one of the most challenging times of my life.

I am in settlements for the next two weeks! We chatted in detail for an hour. I will not be able to respond for at least that long because of the multi-client settlements. Here is copy of my original text  to send or say to them!

I cannot help you right now because I do not know for sure that there are violations of the law. You also said you understood that.

————-

Original review above.

Yes, we talked for about 15 minutes at like 7:30 PM and you were obviously drunk.  I guess it seemed like an hour to you.

Yes, I sent you songs, so what?

I was in crisis and reaching out to you to help me.

Instead of listening to what I had to say, you recommended that I contact a bunch of other lawyers and claim a bunch of other things insofar as discrimination.  I told you Gina and FMLA, and you wanted me to complain about a lot of other things that are ancillary to the meat of my case.

If you need the opinions of other attorneys to decide whether you should take a case, you probably shouldn’t have your own law firm.

I’m the one suffering.  Why should I do the extra work calling other lawyers to send you their opinions.  Are you not capable of forming your own valid opinion on a case without first hearing that several other attorneys are on my side?

Are you not capable of seeking those other opinions yourself?

Today, I wrote you apologizing that we got off on you wrong foot, asking you to reconsider, letting you know that I went and contacted other attorneys on my own, without your help; The response below is all I received in return.

Hope you’re enjoying happy hour!

Cheers!
reviews.ownerResponse:
First, your case had many layers to it. I only take a case when I am certain that there are violations. I spent almost an hour or more on the intake phone call. Then I sent you a link for referrals of up to 100 other labor lawyers for their consideration to help you!

Second, I gave you steps to follow which I sent to you multiple times. Then you began threatening me with bad reviews. After our call, I learned that I was in a multi-client settlement. You then wrote to me up to 63 more times via text message in that one evening as harassment and you laughed or mocked me when I said that my time goes first to current clients.

You first started with sweetness or something similar and also sent songs and then I said I was too busy you got more and more angry. You also stated that you were wasted.

Since then, I blocked you and you wrote today asking for help. To anyone reading this please know that this review is not truthful for a usual exchange with an intake!

reviews.text: Goz Odediran is the lawyer you want on your team if you are a student facing a university disciplinary proceeding. Goz helped our family successfully appeal the dismissal (expulsion) of our son from medical school in the final year of his MD/PhD program. I only wish we had called Goz as soon as the school requested a “meeting” with him. We learned the hard way that universities are expelling students for minor offenses. Do not wait… call Goz right away if you are a student accused of any type of infraction. Do not respond to any school communications yourself – let Goz do the talking. She is excellent.

reviews.text:
There is an old saying: Engineers hide their mistakes under concrete; lawyers behind bars; and physicians, 6 feet under. Yep. I have seen it all. Most lawyers (don’t get me started with health professionals) I know are only interested in your money, not on your case, and certainly not on winning it or looking for the best outcome for you. I’ve seen cases where they will drag the case for months or years until there is no more money left for you to give them, and then they will settle. They do this so they can keep your money flowing to their accounts for as long as they can, piling up “billable hours” and draining you out.
Here is my take, and I really hope this will help you:
As a starting educator I was told, long ago,  I could benefit from having the “*****” or the “**********” on my side, so I could be protected from any legal trouble I could find along the way. Then I learned teachers in Texas have no right to unionize since this is a “right to work state”, so, as it seems, that organizations that offer to “have your back”  are not really empowered, nor they can really help you as well as in right to unionize states. They might just be there, of course I could be wrong, to take (in affordable monthly installments) your money. Being the only possible real benefit they could provide some legal support  and a lawyer to represent you, should the need arise. So this is more like legal insurance,  but don’t assume this lawyers got your back. After all you paid up front for services that might not be what you expected the day when it really counts. In the end, they just have arrangements with some law firms that might not care about your needs, or servicing you,  and they might not, necessarily,  being on your side. They got your money already, so what would they care about you? You are just a discounted-rate-prepaid type of customer.
Anyway, let me go to  point: After looking desperately for a good law firm and an honest attorney I found the right person and law firm: Goz Odediran and her law firm. She listened  about my case, pay real attention to the details, asked me relevant questions and informed of my rights, expectations and possible outcomes. She did not ask for money up front, nor retainers, neither push me to sign a contract. She listened and I felt again as a person, so I could focus on the facts and my defense.
I kept asking questions during the following days and she followed-up informing me
With keen interest on what I have to say. Finally explained the way she worked and the involved costs.
Everything that she promised delivered. Ms. Odediran was there for my every step of the way and we had intense discussions to litigate  my defense. We did preparations for my hearing and she stayed with me, during and after it, providing me with legal strategies to make my case.
The outcome could not be better, and I’m not saying retaining Odediran’s law firm will secure a winning for you, but you will have a capable, honest, professional and dependable attorney on your side, so you can do your due diligence and prepare your case knowing you really have someone standing by you all the way to the end.
One piece of advice: Don’t try to go and deal with your legal matters alone! No matter how smart, savvy or educated you are, you will always require and outside objective perspective, and you won’t have alone by yourself, particularly  when your neck is at stake, no matter, sorry to say, if you are innocent (or not). Remember the old adage: If you are your own lawyer you have a fool for a client.

reviews.text: My son was involved in an incident with his best friend at school from horseplay.  The school didn’t take into account the testimony of the other children who was witness to the incident that my son was involved in and they seem really determined with their assessment.  With Goz’s help and involvement, she advised us how to proceed in the hearing for the incident, what to say or not say to build a case for my son.  She came prepared with prints of the school’s policy and was very articulate in describing why my son’s character from what information we had given her and of the incident proved that the course of action that the school was pursuing, my son didn’t fit the bill of the school’s policy for their course of action.  If the school didn’t change their mind, this could have affected my son’s future and after countless calls with the school and feeling that they didn’t really care to carefully examine what we had found out from the children that were involved, we retained Goz.  First thing she told me is that the school will typically try to push the punishment that wasn’t fit for the “crime” and will basically ignore our pleas, really resonated with me because that was what we went through.  No matter how we explained and told them about the witnesses description of what happened, they seemed to want to continue the push to punish my son unfairly even though he wasn’t the instigator.  I am happy with the results because I don’t think I would have gotten the school to listen to me without an attorney present.  Before this incident, I had total faith in our school and their admins so if you think you can reason with the school regarding what you feel to be an unfair potential disciplinary action and the potential actions don’t fit the crime and you feel that you are not being heard, then I would suggest to think twice about retaining an attorney and Goz is very familiar with the law and school’s policy to help you fight it.  The outcome from our hearing probably saved my son’s future for college.

reviews.text:
My son had an incident in school and the decision was upheld for him to attend DAEP (alternative school placement) even though it was his first disciplinary offense becuase he was being bullied by other students.
In addition to this, my child has an IEP plan due to ADHD, so we had to have an MDR meeting to confirm whether my child’s disability was a manifestation of his actions or the school failure to implement his IEP plan is what caused the action.
I had the initial DAEP placement hearing without Attorney Goz because I thought it would be a conversation where I could talk to the administrators about my child’s past disciplinary records, how well behave he usually is, and that he made a mistake. I had a rude awakening! The committee had simply made up their mind already and didn’t really acknowledge my comments about my son and within a couple of hours, they emailed me a letter that said they upheld the recommendation and will be sending my child to DAEP.
Knowing my child and how he doesn’t thrive in changing environments, I knew this was a bad decision and knew that I needed to continue to advocate for him. I was lost, scared, and felt hopeless at that point. I started googling around for attorneys that would just hear my case so that I can better understand my rights. I then found Attorney Goz website and after reading her bio, I felt like she would understand my situation and would be the right fit to represent us as an educational attorney. I called and left her a message and within less than 24 hours she returned my call. She listened to the entire situation and immediately made me feel comfortable. She was realistic and never promised me that there would be a specific outcome BUT just her confidence and knowledge gave me a peace of mind. I was willing to retain her as our attorney to have her represent us as the MDR meeting.
Once I retained her, she went to work right away and started asking for appropriate documents to build our case. During the meeting, I was in awe with her level of professionalism and presentation she provided to the committee that consisted of over 6+ individuals. The person that recommended my child for DAEP changed her mind about my child attending an alternative placement school after attorney Goz presented all the facts. There were two members of the committee that did not listen well to the presentation and voted against in favor of the school, but attorney Goz redirected them in a very tactful way and asked them to really consider her statements and the facts about the case and to not solely rely on an IEP or FIE plan that was completed well over two years ago. At the conclusion of the meeting, the committee voted in favor of us and will NOT be sending my child to alternative school! I promise you; they would not have come to this decision without having attorney Goz on our side.
Attorney Goz also got the school to implement SEVERAL additional action items to ensure that my child thrives, and their behavior issues are addressed head on. You can tell that she genuinely cares about the education of students and is passionate about what she does. The meeting lasted well over an hour and at no time did she try to rush or make you feel like she had other things to do.
I know I am sharing a lot of personal information and the reason that I am is because I know I won’t be the last person that a school or district will try to take advantage of a child. They will come up with the decision to send them to alternative school because it’s the easiest route to take. I would advocate to please do yourself a favor and ALWAYS get a second opinion. This was the best decision that I made for my child and I would refer anyone that I know to her. My overall experience was extremely positive. Regardless of the outcome, I would have felt the exact same way.

There are not enough words to express my gratitude for Goz! I reached out to Goz when I was wrongly accused of academic dishonesty at my university in Houston, TX. As a PhD student, this accusation was particularly scary, because academic dishonesty is taken so seriously in academia.

Goz's team responded to me within one day and called me back as soon as they received my voice message. After one conversation, I knew that she was incredible, not only as a person but at her job. Goz is open minded, honest, direct, caring, and passionate about her work. She is an incredible listener, and she took detailed notes on everything having to do with my case. When we met after our first meeting, she remembered everything I'd told her from before.

We met for a bit over 2 hours altogether (she schedules meetings for one hour time slots, but during our second meeting, she let me stay on for a bit longer, venting and expressing my anxieties!). Goz patiently explained the entire hearing process to me and advised me about the next steps I should take. It is because of her that I found out I could obtain witness statements, provide video evidence and discuss past problems I had faced with the professor accusing me. Two days before my trial, she even walked me step-by-step through a mock hearing trial, where she listened to my opening statement and gave me advice.

Goz never wavered in her belief and support in me, and this made me feel stronger on the day of my actual hearing. Additionally, Goz had the option of attending the hearing with me for a retainer fee, but because she values honest, she told me she believed I could win on my own. She also reassured me that that if things didn't go my way, she would be there to represent me at the next hearing.

Three days ago, I was informed that I had won my trail! This outcome is 100% because of Goz and her efforts to help me. If anyone has the opportunity to work with her, TAKE IT!!! She represents the best of law, and anyone would be lucky, lucky, lucky to work with her.

reviews.text:
Long read but my real situation:
I was accused of an honor code violation by a professor at a private university: cheating; use of outside resources on an online proctored exam due to excessive eye movement and gazing. I didn’t know what to do other than find someone who did. I searched all Friday morning specifically for a lawyer who dealt with college students who were facing allegations of cheating. I called on Good Friday afternoon, spoke to a live person, and scheduled a consultation with Attorney Goz for the upcoming Monday. I was embarrassed and nervous having to make such a phone call and explain an event timeline of the allegation I was facing. But Goz put me at ease (Is that even such a possible feeling? – I know that is what you’re thinking if you are faced with a similar allegation). She was straightforward from the beginning to the end. She reassured me her thoughts aligned with mine as this allegation was ridiculous. She also told me about a recent case much like mine that her client won! After talking with Goz, I knew she was going to help defend and protect my interests and rights.

An attorney was not allowed in an honor council hearing, according to university policy, which was even more frightening. Attorney Goz assured me that I would be ready to defend myself, even though I didn’t believe that at first, as I was just anxious. She explained her plan of approach for the case and prepared me for it. Goz prepared all documents and a script for my presentation to the honor council. She composed a list of questions and responses if I was asked by the honor council. I emailed Goz many questions late at night (10:30 p.m. and later). She would always respond within 30 minutes. As my allegations were from online exams, the videos were made available to watch. I sent the videos to Goz, and she watched the videos (one test so many times as she knew the videos more than me (one test being 3 ½ hours and the other test being 2 ½ hours). I honestly didn’t watch too much of the videos because I was not a fair judge of myself and couldn’t make myself watch. Goz would have all the times in the exam videos that she was concerned about written down and discussed with me. I then watched those exact time frames that Goz prepared for me, making it easy for me, very helpful, and not so nerve-racking. She made it a simple task of uploading all documents for my presentation to the honor council as everything was labeled and to my liking. Goz met and emailed me each week checking in on me and for updates from university. Before the scheduled hearing (4 months later) Goz would meet with me 3 to 4 times a week and at any time. Goz had me fully prepared and confident to present my defense. When I was first accused, I knew I had to seek an education attorney for higher education students as I had no clue what to do or how to defend myself other than to say I don’t know what I was looking at or I didn’t cheat. Attorney Goz proved to the honor council I did not cheat. I was found NOT GUILTY with Goz doing all the behind-the-scenes work in this case as she could not be present.

I appealed the professor’s allegation of cheating. I was then accused of using an outside resource on a different exam that was 2 months previous by a different professor. This professor was accusing me of using an external device (phone) when in reality it was a light box. I sent the allegation letter and video to Goz. I now had TWO ALLEGATIONS against me. I obtained all necessary documents and pictures requested by Attorney Goz and she went to work. She let me know this was a tougher case therefore she made sure I had it all together. Goz knew how to respond to this 2nd allegation. Long story short, I was found NOT GUILTY of this allegation as well.

Goz represented me on BOTH of my cases with the university I attended, a nursing school program. I was found NOT GUILTY in BOTH CASES from 2 different professors.

I recommend ATTORNEY GOZ for any higher educational matter.

I highly recommend Clifford Swayze for anyone seeking a family law attorney. From start to finish, he demonstrated exceptional professionalism, dedication and integrity. Mr. Swayze worked diligently to minimize costs while still providing top-notch legal support. He was consistently responsive, addressing my concerns promptly and keeping me informed throughout the process. Thanks to his expertise and commitment, there was a favorable outcome in my case. If you are looking for a reliable and effective family law attorney, Clifford Swayze is the one to trust.

I recently had the privilege of working with Clifford Swayze for my divorce case, and I am incredibly grateful for the exceptional legal support I received. From our first consultation, through all the hurdles, to the final resolution, Cliff demonstrated a deep understanding of family law and a genuine commitment to my case.  He took the time to listen and from there guided me through each step in the process.

I cannot put into words how immensely thankful and grateful I am for all that Clifford did to help my sweet mother, guiding her through the most turbulent times of her life–faced with fighting for her life in a battle with cancer and divorce from my father. He was there, advising her every step of the way–oftentimes daily. Clifford was not only my mother’s legal representation, but her friend and confidant.

He was incredibly patient and kind hearted, took the time to listen to get the full story, and then kindly and compassionately responded with the best legal advice.

After my Mom’s passing, he continued to help the family, answering difficult questions with the greatest sensitivity and understanding.

I wholeheartedly recommend Clifford for people seeking legal advice, and I am positive that he’ll help you every step of the way–gathering the facts and listening with intentionality, just like he helped my mother.

I am eternally grateful to you for ALL you did, Clifford.
Cliff was immaculate in helping me with my divorce. Easy communication and effective work. With a little patience he worked his miracle!

Clifford has been absolutely amazing to work with. I had been going through a really annoying divorce process, and was represented poorly by a different lawyer in the area and did not feel like I was being properly defended. I decided to leave her and am so grateful I came across Clifford. I can honestly say he has made this process so much better. He is incredibly kind, a great communicator, and so so nice to work with. You can really tell he cares about what he does and the clients he works with. I HIGHLY recommend him.

Clifford represented me through my custody battle, and I was very pleased with his work. He fought hard for my son and me. Initially, I went with a different attorney, which was a huge mistake. I'm grateful that I found Clifford and that he was able to push to get the results that were in my son's best interest. I’d definitely recommend him to anyone going through a custody case. He is absolutely phenomenal!

I needed an attorney that was aggressive with my case and dishonest spouse, but who would be compassionate with me in navigating a very complicated divorce. I didn't feel like they really took the time to understand my case or me as a person. Once I spoke to Cliff, I instantly knew he was someone who cared about getting a fair settlement for me and my young children.  Cliff took every challenge in stride and was thoughtful in the best strategy based on my goals and what was best for my family. He also presented me with realistic potential outcomes to avoid dragging things out unnecessarily so that I could move forward with my life and begin healing. Cliff was accessible by phone whenever I needed him. I trust him immensely and have recommended him to numerous friends needing a family law attorney.

Mr. Swayze was an answer to prayer.
As heartbreaking as my divorce was for me, it was also very intimidating.
I’m thankful to have had the legal counsel and compassion from such an individual.

From breaking down legal jargon, to helping me understand outcomes of decisions with a long term perspective, again he was an answer to prayer.

My process was much longer than expected (2 years) yet, he stayed by my side.
In the end we were able to celebrate a victory.

The Understanding nature of this man along with his Patience and Kindness are what helped keep me sane through it all.
To have someone walk with you through the storm you didn’t see coming reminds you there is hope for a better future.
Thank you Mr. Swayze

Clifford Swayze handled my divorce in 2015. Mr. Swayze navigated me through the process.  I was very comfortable and trusted his advice.  Anytime I reached out with questions or had a concern, Clifford responded in a timely fashion.  In the end, I felt Clifford Swayze was fair and honest.  So much so, I now intend to hire him to write my prenup for my new wife!.    I will be calling you Cliff.

I hired this amazing attorney back on Dec of 2022 for a child custody case. He layed out the ground work of how hard he will fight to keep my son in my life. (We Won The Case) thanks to his knowledge and experience ! 10/10 I would recommend Any and Everyone To Mr. Swayze to get the job Done ✅ He saved my sons Life 🙏

My divorce to my knowledge was finalized may of 23 come to find out Cliff was dodging calls from not only me but my husbands attorney as well. Our final decree was never signed by me because I never received it from Cliff and we are still married TO THIS DAY…. I tried contacting him along with my husbands attorney and no answers. I just received a google doc in my gmail inbox to FINALLY to sign my final decree….. granted he did get me full custody of my son and handled the case very well but is very unprofessional and a horrible communicator. He did not make me feel heard until I had to hound him to get things done. This has only been my experience and I project wish this on anyone to spend this much money to be divorced to only find out months later your still married. I would love a refund but knowing him and how he handles things it’s not worth it, I probably wouldn’t even get a response anyways.

Clifford Swayze is an outstanding attorney who combines legal expertise with genuine compassion. Had Cliff help me with another case and as always he was outstanding! Definitely recommend!

I could not have asked for a better attorney at one of the most challenging times in my life. Cliff is thorough, patient, knowledgable and encouraging. I highly recommend Cliff to anyone out there that needs family law advice. He's worth every cent.

Can't say enough about the impeccable work and dedication Mr. Swayze put towards our case.

I worked with Cliff on my recent Divorce.  He was a STRONG representative for me and simplifed everything related to the process so that I could be an active participant, with minimized tasks on my part.

Clifford Swayze truly deserves every bit of praise and recognition for his exceptional legal services. His professionalism, empathy, and expertise exceeded my expectations throughout my entire case.

Affordability was a significant concern for me initially, but Clifford made the legal process manageable without compromising the quality of his services. His transparent and fair pricing structure alleviated any financial stress, allowing me to focus on navigating a challenging time in my life.

Attorney Swayze's strategic approach and unwavering commitment to achieving justice for his clients are truly commendable. I wholeheartedly recommend him to anyone in need of legal representation. He is professional and goes above and beyond to advocate for his clients' rights and well-being.

I had Mr. Swayze as my child custody attorney for about 6 months and he kept consistent communication with me about my case the entire time! Mr. Swayze is so good that he  beat 3, yes THREE lawyers in court and got my daughter back to me who I hadn't seen in over 11 months. I had probably one of the most complicated custody cases imaginable.  I really figured that there was not much of a chance. Yet, somehow we did it. I'm astonished and amazed at his ability and professionalism. If I ever need a lawyer again, I will be using his services again.  It's strange to say that a lawyer is my hero, but it's absolutely true.  Thank you again for the hundredth time, you're amazing.

After you spend money on bad attorneys you lose trust in the system. I can 100% tell you that Cliff is your guy. Most attorneys will  give you the run around, that’s not Cliff. Straight forward about the situation regardless if it’s what you want to hear or not from the beginning. Extremely knowledgeable. If you want someone that cares, I would give him a call.

I could not recommend Cliff enough. Cliff was nothing short of professional, knowledgeable and compassionate. I’m very pleased with my experience and outcome!

I reached a time in my first marriage that required a divorce. I feel sorry for anyone that has to cross that same bridge. Cliff Swayze took the time to advise me about the legalities of the situation with compassion. After I became his client Cliff took the time to have brief conversations throughout the process without racking up multiple“15 minute charges.”

I had to travel across the state of Texas to attend my divorce hearing and Cliff did not let me down. He stood beside me at the courthouse even though his Father was laying on his deathbed in the hospital. I feel that this demonstrates his dedication to his clients. The divorce decree was agreed upon and was finalized.

My ex wife agreed to a commitment regarding real estate and failed to comply by the deadline she chose. Cliff represented me again to ensure she complied. The Judge ruled in my favor.

A little time passed and we had a custody dispute. Cliff was there for me. I had to assign a Guardian ad litem and march through the muck of all that. Cliff walked beside me.

If you are reading this I recommend that you do your best to work your marriage out. If that is not possible and you have to walk through a wolf pack Cliff is the shepherd you need!

I hired Clifford for my uncontested divorce and would recommend him to anyone in the future. reviews.text: I completely handed her a case that was already won and she still took half my settlement wouldn't recommend unless you want to split your settlement..
reviews.ownerResponse:
Calia, I don't think you were ever represented by this office, but if you are posting under a false name, I don't know who you are and have no way of addressing your concern any further.

None of my contingency agreements amount to me taking half or even close to half of anyone's settlement, and none of my cases settle without my client's permission and agreement, so if your statement is truthful, you deserve redress. Every single one of my settlement conferences provide an opportunity for clients to address if they are unhappy with their settlement and to explore other options. I've never settled a case if my client wasn't happy with the settlement, especially if, as you claim, you "completely handed" me a case that was "already won."

Please address your concern to the State Bar of Texas Grievance Hotline at (800) 932-1900.

Further, please remove any statement you have made that as factually untrue and defamatory.

reviews.text:
Slaton Schauer Law Firm

I wish I could post more than five stars for this wonderful law firm.
After recently being the executor of my mother’s estate, I realized that my will from 45 years ago was woefully out of date and I needed to get my estate into a trust.  I could not use my mother’s estate firm as they are in California, but they highly recommended this law firm.
When I returned to Texas, I gave them a call and they set up an initial meeting less than a week away. Kim was my first point of contact, and she made sure that I got a good head start on what I needed to consider and bring to the introductory meeting.
I met with Regina a few days later and she walked me through the entire process under Texas law and explained every detail I asked about in a way that made sense to me. I walked away from that meeting with a much broader view of how I wanted to structure my trust.
I made what I thought was a comprehensive outline of what I wanted and submitted it by email. Kim got back to me a few days later and we walked through my outline and she pressed me for as much detail as possible in order to have the finished trust meet my expectations.
In the end, the signing meeting went flawlessly.  We didn’t need to change a single word and I cannot imagine how it could have been better planned, constructed or executed.  From start to finish, the process and end result were clear, concise and exactly what I wanted. Both Regina and Kim are exceptional at what they do and how they do it.  I look forward to working with them over the years as life changes things.
(Six stars…)

reviews.text:
Regina Schauer is the best!
I attended 3 of her and Andrew Slaton's seminars before hiring Regina to completely rewrite my Trust.
Because I have a complicated family, I do  not want to leave any room for misunderstanding when I pass.  My estate is small.  But I am aware that family members can get hurt over small things during grief and stressful situations like the death of a loved one.  So, I'm trying to prevent any conflict when I am no longer here.
Because I have witnessed family disagreements, I wanted to make sure my affairs are clear.  So, in 2014 I developed, with an attorney, my Trust.  However, over the years I have altered this Trust.  I find such matters confusing.  I'm always open to learning, thus I continued to read and attend various seminars.  I could not believe how many shortcomings were in my Trust.  So, I continued to attend seminars and read and study the details.  Finally, I decided to totally rewrite my Trust with Regina's help.  The difference is amazing.
My new Trust is so organized.  All possibilities are covered.  There should be no confusion.
I found Regina patient in explaining complicated areas.  Her office is low stress and pleasant.  She is knowledgeable and thorough.  I left with an organized binder and confident I have done everything possible.  I am motivated to get all of my paperwork in order.  I know if I have questions I can call Regina's office and get answers.  It is one of the best things a person can leave to their loved ones, a roadmap to handling property, financial affairs, and clear direction to prevent family disputes and hurt feelings when you are no longer here to explain and love them.

My goodness, please take a moment to consider this. Lytza is exceptionally skilled in her profession. Having her as my attorney has been the best decision I could have made. As a first-time offender, I was arrested and charged with two Class A Misdemeanors (Assault Bodily Harm to Family Member and Terroristic Threat) while defending myself against ongoing domestic violence, which was, of course, quite bizarre. From the very first day, Lytza not only listened to my side of the story, but she also supported me with understanding, empathy, and a non-judgmental attitude, making me feel valued. Her defense strategy is unparalleled. During my time of emotional trauma and distress, Lytza consistently delivered on her promises. Thanks to her efforts, both cases and charges were dismissed, and I was found not guilty, as it became evident that I should not have been arrested. Lytza meticulously gathered all prior records and history of domestic violence, and her insights were revealed in court. I was granted early expungement approval, all thanks to Lytza's diligence. She truly excels in her field. Trust me when I say she knows what she is doing and holds everyone accountable. As a woman who has endured such situations for some time, Lytza also helped me rediscover myself and my worth. I am incredibly grateful for both her support and divine intervention, as many in similar situations do not have the same outcome. I would choose Lytza repeatedly without hesitation. Choose an attorney who will not view you merely as a number or statistic, as the justice system often does, and Lytza proved them wrong. You will not regret choosing Lytza. She is dedicated and exceptional. Thank you, Lytza, for everything. You are truly an angel and a blessing. I cannot thank you enough. Lytza was very patient and flexible and handled me as a client with care. I will never forget during a tough time that I had went through I Will never forget how she made me feel and she treated me. SHE is WORTH EVERY PENNY!! #Justice #Was #Served

If you're anything like myself, when you're looking for legal representation chances are that you want someone that gives you the best chance, correct? Maybe someone that cares? How about an attorney who also has a team of folks within the walls of their office who are competent, thoughtful, and follow through?

This place checks all the boxes.

Now, think about this: How about hiring a defense attorney who used to work as a county prosecutor? Smartest thing you could ever do.

I'm not a good criminal, but I'm good at getting caught. For some reason. And because of this, if I so much as hear the word "attorney" I butt in to tell them about Lisa. If I'm part of the conversation or not. Don't care. You're gonna hear about this lady that I've come to love and respect.

Talk to everyone else and then go see her. You'll understand what I'm saying.

You'll hire her. You'll be more than satisfied.

You're welcome.

reviews.text: If you like paying over 20k for a failed mediation and having your bill increase with zero work being done, then being left to represent yourself these guys are great! Horrible experience with Krista Banda.  My case clearly should have begun after I sought severe grief therapy, I'd lost my son and a month later his ex took his daughter away from us.  It was too close to both losses for me to fare well in court, and I didn't.

I paid them $15,000 as a retainer, but a rapid fire of extensive information either in their possession or not relevant.

There were four paralegals working on my case in addition to having co-counsel in court.  I did not even get asked if l wanted to pay for that.  Then she twice tried to charge me for communication from opposing council, demanding his court fees when I had 30 days to respond.  She has nothing to do with my paying him.  The second co-counsel actually handed me several papers to sign regarding our financial situation as i was waiting to be called into the courtroom. She served no purpose nor did the financial paperwork I didn't fill out.
Total was $22,000 and no tests run to prove my assertions of abuse.
Please don't expect a fair assessment of your case.  The trauma this entire event has caused me is unimaginable and the excess charges were improper.
All of the above is my perception and opinion of this situation.  Please don't try to go to family court when you haven't grieved.  All the above is my interpretation.
